elements.text: Interest as collateral as it does on underwriting the Borrower’s creditworthiness. The quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a Cooperative Share Loan can be impacted by the financial stability and viability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: particular project, among other project characteristics.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The conclusion that a Cooperative Share Loan is acceptable to Freddie Mac must be based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the determination that the Borrower is creditworthy and the Cooperative Interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation is adequate collateral for the Cooperative Share Loan transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Seller approval for Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be approved to sell Cooperative Share Loans, Sellers/Servicers must meet minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements to be considered for approval to sell Cooperative Share Loans, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Possess sufficient expertise with Cooperative Projects and Cooperative Share Loans,
elements.label: text

elements.text: along with the capacity and resources to ensure that the Cooperative Project is likely to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be successful does not expose Freddie Mac to material credit risk and meets eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Possess substantial experience in the geographic market in which Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans are being originated
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Demonstrate volume of Cooperative Share Loans correlates to years of experience
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Engage dedicated project review staff with experience in underwriting Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects, and retain legal counsel (or have access to legal counsel) familiar with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Projects and Cooperative Project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Maintain written policies and procedures consistent with Freddie Mac’s project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements. The policies and procedures must address pre-sale and owner-occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, declining market trends, project completion and any other issues that layer
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional risk for Cooperative Share Loans. The policies and procedures for identifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Share Loans to be sold to Freddie Mac may be no less stringent than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures for the Seller’s investment decisions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Maintain controls for monitoring performance of Cooperative Share Loans it originates
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., a 60-day Delinquency within the first three months or any 90 or more day
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency ever). Sellers must also maintain policies and procedures to implement
elements.label: text

elements.text: default management corrective actions that also evaluate and monitor the performance of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Share Loans following implementation of such corrective action.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must not be aware of any quality or servicing issues related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans it originates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Provide project-related reporting and any project underwriting documentation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project Documents upon request from Freddie Mac or request the
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation from the Cooperative Corporation on Freddie Mac’s behalf, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Glossary definitions and other terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Glossary definitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller should be familiar with Freddie Mac’s Glossary definitions of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Glossary definitions for Critical Repairs and Routine Repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Critical Repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (includes material
elements.label: text

elements.text: deficiencies and significant
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred maintenance)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repairs and replacements that significantly impact the safety,
elements.label: text

elements.text: soundness, structural integrity or habitability of the project’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: building(s) and/or that impact unit values, financial viability or
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability of the project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: These include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Material deficiencies which, if left uncorrected, have the
elements.label: text

elements.text: potential to result in or contribute to critical element or system
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure within one year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any mold, water intrusions or potentially damaging leaks to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project’s building(s) that have not been repaired
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advanced physical deterioration
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any project that failed to pass state, county, or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdictional mandatory inspections and/or certifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific to structural soundness, safety, and habitability; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any unfunded repairs costing more than $10,000 per unit that
elements.label: text

elements.text: should be undertaken within the next 12 months (does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: include repairs made by the unit owner or repairs funded through
elements.label: text

elements.text: a special assessment)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Examples of some items to consider include, but are not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: sea walls, elevators, waterproofing, stairwells, balconies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: foundation, electrical systems, parking structures or other load￾bearing structures.
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Routine Repairs These repairs are not considered to be critical and include work that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preventative in nature or part of normal capital replacements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., focused on keeping the project fully functioning and
elements.label: text

elements.text: serviceable); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accomplished within the project’s normal operating budget or
elements.label: text

elements.text: through special assessments that are within guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Other terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following additional terms are used in this chapter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: B
elements.label: text

elements.text: Blanket Mortgage A Mortgage secured by a Cooperative Project; is also referred
elements.label: text

elements.text: to as an “underlying mortgage.”
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Established Cooperative Project is a Cooperative Project in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Project is complete, and is not subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional phasing, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold Shares) has
elements.label: text

elements.text: turned control of the Cooperative Corporation over to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: H
elements.label: text

elements.text: Holder of Unsold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any of the following persons or Entities that (a) hold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Shares in Cooperative Units, (b) except as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described below, do not intend to cause the Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: to be occupied and (c) intend to lease the Cooperative Units or
elements.label: text

elements.text: keep the Cooperative Units vacant:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sponsor/developer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Persons/entities that the sponsor/developer designates as
elements.label: text

elements.text: holders of unsold Cooperative Shares; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsequent transferees of such unsold Cooperative Shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: which retain their character as unsold Cooperative Shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: until one of the occupancy events described below occurs
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this definition, intending to cause the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit to be occupied means occupancy by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: persons holding the Cooperative Shares in the Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., subsequent transferees) the principals of an Entity holding
elements.label: text

elements.text: such Cooperative Shares or any family members of the persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: holding the Cooperative Shares, employees or persons affiliated
elements.label: text

elements.text: with such Entities holding the Cooperative Shares.
elements.label: text

elements.text: N
elements.label: text

elements.text: New Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: A New Cooperative Project is a Cooperative Project in which:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Project is not complete, or is subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional phasing, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold Shares) has
elements.label: text

elements.text: not turned control of the Cooperative Corporation over to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.2: Cooperative Project review and general
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Cooperative Project eligibility requirements (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must determine compliance with Freddie Mac’s Cooperative Project review and
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements in this section. This section contains the following subsections:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General Cooperative Project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac right to review Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cooperative Project review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Cooperative Share Loans delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the requirements in Section 5705.7 relating to Exempt from Review, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must ensure that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Cooperative Project, the Cooperative Unit and Cooperative Share Loan comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following sections in this chapter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project eligibility (see Section 5705.4)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal requirements for New Cooperative Projects (see Section 5705.6)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The project must not be an ineligible Cooperative Project (see Section 5705.3)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller must review and determine that a Cooperative Project complies with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s requirements as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project review expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Type of Cooperative Project Expiration of project review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Established Cooperative Project Within one year prior to the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: New and Newly Converted Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 180 days prior to the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Cooperative Project does not meet Freddie Mac’s project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Note Date, the Seller may deliver the Cooperative Share Loan at the time the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project complies with all of the project eligibility requirements as long as all
elements.label: text

elements.text: other applicable requirements have been met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Cooperative Project, Cooperative Project Documents and Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents must comply with all applicable laws, ordinances and regulations for the State
elements.label: text

elements.text: and local jurisdiction in which the project is located, and all documents must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforceable under the laws, ordinances and regulations of the applicable jurisdiction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General Cooperative Project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must also review and determine compliance with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The project must have insurance that complies with the applicable requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 4703
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Cooperative Unit and the Cooperative Project must be covered by a title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy that complies with requirements of Chapter 4702
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Cooperative Project and Cooperative Unit must comply with all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Topic 5600
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Cooperative Project must be designed primarily for residential use
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Cooperative Share Loan must comply with all applicable requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. A Cooperative Share Loan must be delivered to Freddie Mac within 120 days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Date. If the Cooperative Share Loan is not delivered within 120 days after the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, Seller must update the project review and eligibility determination prior to delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. The Seller must have policies and procedures in place and take appropriate steps to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that the Cooperative Unit, Cooperative Share Loan and Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with applicable requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. The Seller must retain all documentation related to the review of the Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request, the Seller must provide the project information and documentation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac right to review Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to conduct its own review of the Cooperative Project for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans delivered to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.3: Ineligible Cooperative Projects (09/30/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ownership of Cooperative Project land and Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative hotel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project with multi-dwelling units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project with excessive commercial or non-residential space
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tenancy-in-Common Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timeshare Cooperative Project with segmented ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Houseboat Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ownership and use of Common Elements in Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project with excessive single-investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Continuing Care Retirement Community (CCRC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Projects composed of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project with mandatory dues or similar membership fees for use of Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as clubhouses or recreational facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limited equity Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasing Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sponsor/developer interest in Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project in need of Critical Repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project with an evacuation order
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Projects terminating or involved in insolvency proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Cooperative Share Loans delivered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 5705.7 relating to Exempt from Review,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans in any of the following types of Cooperative Projects are ineligible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: sale to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Ownership of Cooperative Project land and Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Cooperative Corporation that does not own:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The land on which the Cooperative Project is located in fee simple; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Units in the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Cooperative hotel
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any project that is a cooperative hotel or similar type of transient housing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects that have one or more of the following characteristics are considered a cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: hotel, or similar type of transient housing, and are ineligible projects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Projects that include hotel type services and characteristics, such as registration services,
elements.label: text

elements.text: rentals of Cooperative Units on a daily basis and restrictions on interior decorating
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Projects that are conversions of a hotel (or a conversion of a similar type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: transient housing) unless the Cooperative Project was a Gut Rehabilitation and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resulting Cooperative Units no longer have the characteristics of a hotel or similar type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: transient housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Projects with mandatory or voluntary rental-pooling and revenue-sharing agreements (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar agreements that restrict the Shareholder’s ability to occupy the Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as blackout dates and occupancy limits) to assure an inventory of Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: for rent on a frequent basis, such as daily, weekly, monthly or seasonally
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Projects that are licensed as a hotel, motel or similar type of transient
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are examples of personalized services and centralized systems that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: common red flags of a Condominium Hotel or similar type of transient housing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Personalized services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Daily cleaning services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Porters/luggage service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Room service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Centralized systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any central telephone service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Central key systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Shareholders of Cooperative Corporations in Cooperative Projects in resort locations rent
elements.label: text

elements.text: their units (either individually or through a rental management company) on a short-term
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis, the project has personalized services and/or the project has centralized systems, this
elements.label: text

elements.text: alone does not indicate that the project is to be considered a cooperative hotel. Sellers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully analyze all the characteristics of the project and related information to determine if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project is a cooperative hotel. Related informational resources may include, but are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, Cooperative Project Documents (e.g., by-laws, project budgets and financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements), offering statements (or their equivalent) and marketing materials, websites,
elements.label: text

elements.text: contracts for sale and appraisal reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Cooperative Project with multi-dwelling units
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project that permits a Cooperative Interest in more than one dwelling unit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: with ownership of all owned Cooperative Units or Cooperative Shares financed by a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Cooperative Project with excessive commercial or non-residential space
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project in which more than 35% of its total above and below grade square
elements.label: text

elements.text: footage (or more than 35% of the total above and below grade square footage of the building
elements.label: text

elements.text: in which the Cooperative Project is located) is used as commercial or non-residential space.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In calculating the amount of commercial or non-residential space, Sellers must determine:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total square footage of the project (or the building in which the project is located)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The square footage of the commercial or non-residential space; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The residential space square footage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller will then divide the total commercial or non-residential square footage by the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: square footage of the project or building to determine the total amount of commercial or non￾residential space. Below is a table illustrating what must be included or may be excluded
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the calculation of commercial or non-residential space.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excessive commercial or non-residential space
elements.label: text

elements.text: Type of commercial or non-residential space Include in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation?
elements.label: text

elements.text: Retail and other commercial or non-residential space (for
elements.label: text

elements.text: example, restaurants and stores)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residential rental apartments, hotels, motels and other similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: types of space, although such space may have residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Non-residential space that the Cooperative Corporation does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: own but that is owned by a private individual or entity outside of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Corporation (for example, private fitness
elements.label: text

elements.text: facilities that are membership-based rather than owned by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation for the sole use of the Shareholders)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yes
elements.label: text

elements.text: The total square footage of commercial or non-residential space
elements.label: text

elements.text: even when the Cooperative Corporation representing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shareholders is different from the association representing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: commercial owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Commercial parking facilities No
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excessive commercial or non-residential space
elements.label: text

elements.text: Type of commercial or non-residential space Include in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation?
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project Amenities and facilities that are residential in nature,
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned by the Cooperative Corporation and allocated for the sole
elements.label: text

elements.text: use of the Shareholders
elements.label: text

elements.text: No
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Tenancy-in-Common Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project owned by several owners as tenants-in-common. Individuals have an
elements.label: text

elements.text: undivided interest in the Cooperative Project (including the Cooperative Units) and land on
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Cooperative Project is located and may or may not have the right of exclusive
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupancy of a specific Cooperative Unit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Timeshare Cooperative Project with segmented ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project in which there is an arrangement under which a Shareholder receives
elements.label: text

elements.text: an interest in real estate and the right to use a Cooperative Unit, Amenities or both for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified period and on a recurring basis (such as the 15th week of the year) or ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is for a limited period (such as for the subsequent five years).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Houseboat Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project composed of boats that have been designed or modified to be used
elements.label: text

elements.text: primarily as dwelling units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Ownership and use of Common Elements in Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as stated below, a Cooperative Corporation must be the sole owner of and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shareholders must have the sole right to the use of the Common Elements, including all
elements.label: text

elements.text: buildings, roads, parking, facilities and Amenities. The sponsor/developer must not retain
elements.label: text

elements.text: any ownership interest in the Common Elements, facilities and Amenities, except as a Holder
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Unsold Shares.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project that shares Amenities with one or more other residential projects is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible if the projects share the Amenities (e.g., recreational or fitness facilities, swimming
elements.label: text

elements.text: pools and clubhouses) for the sole use of the Shareholders and unit owners, if applicable. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: term “residential projects” includes only residential Condominium Projects, Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects and Planned Unit Developments (PUDs). The residential projects must have an
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement specifying:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A description of the shared Amenities and the terms of Shareholders’ and unit owners’
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted use of the shared Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. How the shared Amenities will be funded, managed and maintained; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The method for resolving disputes between the projects regarding the shared Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Common Elements, including Amenities such as parking and recreational facilities, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be subject to a lease between the Cooperative Corporation (as lessee) and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: sponsor/developer or any affiliate of the sponsor/developer (as lessor).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Parking provided under commercial leases or permit arrangements with parties unrelated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the developer are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Cooperative Project in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project in which: (i) the Cooperative Corporation is named as a party to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pending litigation or the Seller discovers that the Cooperative Corporation is a party to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) proceeding, such as arbitration or mediation, or (ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: the project sponsor or developer is named as a party to pending litigation or the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: discovers that the project sponsor or developer is a party in an ADR proceeding and, in either
elements.label: text

elements.text: case, the dispute relates to the safety, structural soundness, functional use or habitability of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller determines that the pending litigation or ADR proceeding involves only minor
elements.label: text

elements.text: matters that do not affect the safety, structural soundness, functional use or habitability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project, the Cooperative Project is eligible if the litigation or ADR proceeding is
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The litigation amount is known, the insurance company has committed to provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: defense and the litigation amount is covered by the insurance policy;
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The litigation amount is unknown, the Seller has documented the Mortgage file with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the complaint, or the most recent amended complaint, and with an attorney letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: that supports the Seller’s determination that the litigation involves minor matters. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney letter must state all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. The reason for the litigation;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. That the insurance company has committed to provide the defense; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. That any potential monetary judgment against the Cooperative Corporation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: settlement with the Cooperative Corporation, including punitive damages, will likely
elements.label: text

elements.text: be covered by the Cooperative Corporation’s insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the attorney letter indicates that the matter will not likely be fully covered by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation’s insurance policy, then the Cooperative Project is
elements.label: text

elements.text: ineligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The matter involves any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. A non-monetary neighbor dispute or right of quiet enjoyment, whether litigated or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: an ADR proceeding;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. A dispute in which the Cooperative Corporation is the plaintiff in a foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: action;
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. A dispute in which the Cooperative Corporation is the plaintiff in the litigation or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: party to an ADR proceeding and is seeking reimbursement for expenditures made to
elements.label: text

elements.text: repair the Cooperative Project’s component(s). The expenditures may have included
elements.label: text

elements.text: items that related to the safety, structural soundness, functional use or habitability of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the project, provided that the repair permanently resolved the defect or issue, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenditures did not significantly impact the financial stability or future solvency of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Corporation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The estimated or known amount in the dispute in the litigation or ADR proceeding is
elements.label: text

elements.text: known and is not covered by the insurance policy but is not expected to exceed 10% of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Project’s funded reserves, provided that use of the project’s funded
elements.label: text

elements.text: reserves to pay for project litigation or dispute resolution does not violate the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: jurisdiction’s laws and regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must retain documentation to support its analysis that the reason for the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets Freddie Mac’s requirements for minor matters as described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Cooperative Project with excessive single-investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any Cooperative Project in which a single entity owns Cooperative Shares that represent
elements.label: text

elements.text: more than the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excessive single investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Number of units in the project Total number of units owned by
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual or single entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Two to four One
elements.label: text

elements.text: Five to 20 Two
elements.label: text

elements.text: 21 or more 20%
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The following may be excluded from the single investor concentration calculation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Vacant Cooperative Units being actively marketed by the sponsor/developer (or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Holder of Unsold Shares). Any units leased by the sponsor/developer must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the calculation of the developer’s percentage of ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Cooperative Units that a non-profit entity controls or owns for the purpose of
elements.label: text

elements.text: providing affordable housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Cooperative Units held in affordable housing programs (including units subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-eviction rent regulation codes), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. Cooperative Units retained for workforce housing by higher-education institutions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. For purchase transactions, a project with single investor concentration greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified above will be eligible provided:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. The purchase transaction will result in a reduction of the single investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. The single investor must not own more than 49% of the units in the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. The Seller obtains evidence that the single investor is marketing units for sale with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the goal to decrease the single investor concentration to 20% or less of the units in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. The single investor is current on all Maintenance Fees and assessments; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: v. There are no planned or current special assessments in the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. When a sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold Shares) has been prevented from selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Interests due to the need to comply with rent control or tenant-protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws, the 20% single entity ownership limitation may be increased to 49% if all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. The Cooperative Interests owned by the sponsor/developer in excess of 20% must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject to rent control or tenant-protection laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. The Seller must retain documentation, such as a regulation agreement, evidencing
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Cooperative Units retained by the sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shares) are subject to rent control or tenant-protection laws; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. The Seller meets one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: A. Seller must document that the rental income from the tenant-protected and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: leased Cooperative Units owned by the sponsor/developer is sufficient to cover
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Pro Rata Share of the Cooperative Project’s financial obligations that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable to the tenant-protected, leased, and vacant Cooperative Units; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: B. If the rental income from the tenant-protected and/or leased Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned by the sponsor/developer is not sufficient to cover the Pro Rata Share of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Project’s financial obligations that are applicable to the tenant
elements.label: text

elements.text: protected, leased, and vacant units, the sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shares) must have demonstrated the ability and willingness to meet any shortfall
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the payment of the Pro Rata Share of the Cooperative Project’s financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations that are applicable to these units. In such cases, the sponsor/developer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or Holder of Unsold Shares) must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be current on all financial obligations for the subject Cooperative Project and
elements.label: text

elements.text: on all financial obligations relating to any other project in which they own or
elements.label: text

elements.text: hold more than 10% of the Cooperative Shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have sufficient funds to meet any shortfall in the payment of the Pro Rata
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share of the Cooperative Project’s financial obligations that are applicable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the tenant-protected, leased and vacant Cooperative Units; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide financial statements of the Cooperative Corporation that indicate
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial stability for the Cooperative Project with no negative impact to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project due to sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold Shares)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Continuing Care Retirement Community (CCRC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A CCRC is a residential Cooperative Project specifically designed to meet the evolving
elements.label: text

elements.text: health and housing needs of seniors. Unlike age-restricted communities, residents in CCRCs
elements.label: text

elements.text: sign a contract in advance for a lifetime commitment of care from the facility, regardless of
elements.label: text

elements.text: future health or housing needs. CCRCs may also be known as Life-Care Facilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Cooperative Projects composed of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project composed of one or more Manufactured Homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m) Cooperative Project with mandatory dues or similar membership fees for use of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amenities such as clubhouses or recreational facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project with mandatory dues or similar membership fees, including initiation
elements.label: text

elements.text: or joining fees, that allows for the use of Amenities, such as clubhouses or recreational
elements.label: text

elements.text: facilities, unless both of the following are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Corporation solely owns the Amenities; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Only Shareholders are members, with full rights and privileges to the use of these
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amenities being the primary benefit of membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) Limited equity Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project that limits gain from appreciation upon resale of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shares associated with the Cooperative Unit, except as permitted for Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans subject to stock transfer fees under Section 5705.5(f). Cooperative Projects located on
elements.label: text

elements.text: land owned by Community Land Trusts are also ineligible Cooperative Projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (o) Leasing Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Project that involves the leasing of both the land and the improvements to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project including the Common Elements, even if the Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: owns part of the building.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (p) Sponsor/developer interest in Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: The sponsor/developer (or any Holder of Unsold Shares) cannot retain any ownership interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Cooperative Project except for its interest in any unsold Cooperative Units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (q) Project in need of Critical Repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans secured by Cooperative Interests in Cooperative Projects in need of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Critical Repairs, as defined in the Glossary, are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For both current and planned (i.e., Shareholders approved but the board has not initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection yet) special assessments, Seller must obtain and review the following information
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each special assessment to determine if the funds are for a Critical Repair:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purpose of the special assessment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the special assessment was approved,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original amount of the special assessment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount remaining to be collected, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The expected date the special assessment will be paid in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a structural and/or mechanical inspection has been completed within 3 years of Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: project review date, Seller must review that inspection report. There must not be any Critical
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repairs needed, as well as no current evacuation orders or similar regulatory actions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects in need of Critical Repairs remain ineligible until the required repairs and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection report have been completed and documented. Sellers must review an engineer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: report or substantially similar document to determine that the repairs resolved the building’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: safety, soundness, structural integrity, or habitability concerns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If damage or deferred maintenance is isolated to one or a few units and does not affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall safety, soundness, structural integrity, or habitability of the project, then this project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirement does not apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This requirement does not apply to Routine Repairs, as defined in the Glossary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may need to review a combination of documents to determine a project meets our
elements.label: text

elements.text: physical condition requirements. Some examples include but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative board meeting minutes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Engineer’s reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Structural and/or mechanical inspection reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reserve studies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ List of necessary repairs provided by the Cooperative Corporation or management
elements.label: text

elements.text: company, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other substantially similar documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: This list is not prescriptive or exhaustive. Sellers are responsible for determining which
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents they need to review to ensure compliance with this requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (r) Project with an evacuation order
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project with an evacuation order due to an unsafe condition, either for a partial or total
elements.label: text

elements.text: evacuation of the project’s building(s), is ineligible until the unsafe condition has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: remediated and the building(s) is safe for occupancy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (s) Cooperative Projects terminating or involved in insolvency proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Corporation must not be the subject of any action that would cause the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project to cease to exist, including termination, deconversion or dissolution of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the corporation’s legal structure. In addition, a Cooperative Corporation must not be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject of a voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy, insolvency, liquidation or receivership
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings or any substantially similar action under State or federal law. This includes a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation that has voted or is in the process of voting on any of the actions or
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.4: Cooperative Project eligibility (03/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains the following subsections:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project completion requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Owner-occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project budget requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financial obligations of Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Market acceptance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum number of Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subsidies or other benefits for Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for the underlying Blanket Mortgage for Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prior Cooperative Project financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Cooperative Share Loans delivered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 5705.7 for Exempt from Review, the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project must comply with all of the following eligibility requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cooperative Project completion requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project, including all Cooperative Units and Common Elements within the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project, must be complete and cannot be subject to additional phasing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: annexation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Owner-occupancy requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Interests (i.e., both ownership and the accompanying occupancy rights) that
elements.label: text

elements.text: represent at least 50% of the total number of Cooperative Units in the project must have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold and conveyed or, for New Cooperative Projects (as defined in Section 5705.1(c)(ii)),
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be under contract for sale to Shareholders who occupy their Cooperative Units as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Primary Residence or second home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Cooperative Project budget requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project’s budget must meet all the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Financial strength and viability of Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must underwrite the Cooperative Project to determine the financial strength and
elements.label: text

elements.text: viability of the Cooperative Project. If the most recent budget is not available, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: may rely on a review of the Cooperative Corporation’s most recent audited financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements or corporate tax returns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project’s most recent operating budget, audited financial statements or
elements.label: text

elements.text: corporate tax returns must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be consistent with the nature of the Cooperative Project;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Except as stated in Section 5705.4(c)(ii) below, provide for adequate cash flow to
elements.label: text

elements.text: service the current debt and operating expenses; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide for adequate replacement and operating reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an existing building that is converted to a Cooperative Project and is undergoing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: has undergone a Non-Gut Rehabilitation, the sponsor/developer must have contributed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the replacement reserve fund for the expired life of the components that were used prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the conversion of the building(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Negative cash flow of Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: The negative cash flow for the present year will not exceed 5% of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project’s annual operating budget as demonstrated by the most recent audited financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements, provided that the prior year’s cash flow was not negative by more than 5%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The negative cash flow must be attributable to an isolated expense, or the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project must have supplemental income from sources (e.g., stock transfer fee/flip taxes or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments), that demonstrates the Cooperative Project has adequate cash flow to service
elements.label: text

elements.text: all obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the negative cash flow is attributable to tenant-protected, leased and vacant
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Units, the sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold Shares) must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be current on all financial obligations for the subject Cooperative Project and on all
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial obligations relating to any other Cooperative Project in which they own or
elements.label: text

elements.text: hold more than 10% of the Cooperative Shares;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not have pledged any Cooperative Shares of the Cooperative Project as security for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any loan other than to secure the financing obtained to acquire the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide financial statements of the Cooperative Corporation that indicate financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: stability for the Cooperative Project with no negative impact to the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project due to negative cash flow related to sponsor/developer (or Holder of Unsold
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shares) ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must retain documentation that demonstrates the Cooperative Project has adequate
elements.label: text

elements.text: cash flow to service all of its obligations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Financial obligations of Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project must have good financial management, including the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Payment of Blanket Mortgage, taxes and insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Corporation has not been 30 or more days delinquent on any payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: due under any underlying Blanket Mortgage or for taxes, insurance and other financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations in the last 12 months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Maintenance Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the Shareholders are more than 60 days delinquent in the payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Maintenance Fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the Shareholders are more than 60 days delinquent in the payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: of each special assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Market acceptance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project must be located in an area in which there is a demonstrated market
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptance of the cooperative form of ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Minimum number of Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project must consist of two or more 1-unit dwellings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Subsidies or other benefits for Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Cooperative Project is the recipient of any subsidies or similar benefits (e.g., tax or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment abatements) that will be reduced or eliminated within three years of the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date of the Cooperative Share Loan, Seller must assess the impact that the elimination of
elements.label: text

elements.text: such benefits will have on the Cooperative Project and include any higher monthly fees in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s monthly liabilities when qualifying the Borrower for the Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Requirements for the underlying Blanket Mortgage for Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Blanket Mortgage for the Cooperative Project may be either a conventional mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: an FHA-Insured Mortgage that has an interest rate that is at the market rate and not at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsidized or otherwise reduced rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Blanket Mortgage is a balloon mortgage, the remaining term must be at least six
elements.label: text

elements.text: months. If the Blanket Mortgage is an adjustable-rate balloon mortgage with a remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: term between six months and two years, then the current interest rate may not be subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: an interest rate adjustment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Prior Cooperative Project financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that the Cooperative Project’s Blanket Mortgage is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In compliance with the requirements imposed by the mortgagee of the underlying Blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Blanket Mortgage includes a due-on-encumbrance clause and the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project is located in a State in which Cooperative Share Loans are considered to be an
elements.label: text

elements.text: encumbrance on the Cooperative Project, the mortgagee of the Blanket Mortgage must
elements.label: text

elements.text: consent to the Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.5: Cooperative Share Loan eligibility (03/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Occupancy requirement for Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan-to-value (LTV) ratios for Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower qualification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maximum Pro Rata Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Subordinate financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Share Loans subject to stock transfer fee (“flip tax”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Share Loan lien priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Corporation’s approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project that is not a Cooperative Housing Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Cooperative Share Loans must comply with the following eligibility requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Occupancy requirement for Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Shareholder must occupy the Cooperative Unit as a Primary Residence or second home.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Shareholder must have a right to occupy the Cooperative Unit pursuant to a Proprietary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lease for a period of time that extends at least to the maturity date of the Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Loan-to-value (LTV) ratios for Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: The LTV ratio is determined by dividing the original UPB of the Cooperative Share Loan by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the lower of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The sale price; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The appraised value of the Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Cooperative Unit’s Pro Rata Share of the Cooperative Corporation’s debt is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: included in the calculation of the LTV ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Borrower qualification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Cooperative Unit Maintenance Fees include unit utility charge, the Maintenance Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be reduced by the documented amount of unit utility charges that are included, prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculating:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Housing expense-to-income ratio; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Debt-to-income ratio
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Maximum Pro Rata Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Pro Rata Share related to the Cooperative Unit must not exceed 35% of the sum of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit’s Pro Rata Share plus the lower of the (i) sales price or (ii) appraised value
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Cooperative Interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, the Pro Rata Share may be increased to 40% when there are significant and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented compensating factors to support the use of the higher percentage, such as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project in strong financial condition with significant cash reserves and good
elements.label: text

elements.text: cash flow.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Cooperative Corporation has obtained a line of credit, the Seller must include the full
elements.label: text

elements.text: available amount of the line of credit as part of the Cooperative Corporation’s debt (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Blanket Mortgage and, if applicable, second mortgage) when calculating the maximum Pro
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rata Share.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table provides an example of how to calculate the Pro Rata Share:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculating Pro Rata Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Variable Example value
elements.label: text

elements.text: Blanket Mortgage balance $4,000,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate financing balance $1,000,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Shares issued and outstanding 10,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject unit’s Cooperative Shares 40
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sales price/appraised value of the Cooperative Interest (not including
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Pro Rata Share of the Cooperative Corporation’s debt)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pro Rata Cooperative Share of the Cooperative Corporation’s debt $20,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Cooperative Corporation’s total debt = [Blanket Mortgage balance] plus [Subordinate
elements.label: text

elements.text: financing balance]
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Pro rata debt by share = [Cooperative Corporation’s total debt] divided by
elements.label: text

elements.text: [Cooperative Shares issued and outstanding]
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Pro Rata Cooperative Share of the Cooperative Corporation’s debt = [Pro rata debt by
elements.label: text

elements.text: share] times [Subject unit’s Cooperative Shares]
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Pro rata % of value/price = [pro rata $ amount] divided by [pro rata $ amount +
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraised value]
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Subordinate financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subordinate financing is permitted for Cooperative Units that are Primary Residences.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Cooperative Share Loans subject to stock transfer fee (“flip tax”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans that are subject to the payment of a flip tax are permitted so long as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Project Documents permit the imposition of a flip tax and provide for one of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller is exempt from paying the flip tax if the Seller acquires the Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a result of a foreclosure, in a transfer by the Shareholder in lieu of foreclosure, or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other transfer of the Shareholder’s interest in the Cooperative Unit in full or partial
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfaction of the Shareholder’s obligations under the Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The flip tax is payable when the sales price exceeds the existing Shareholder’s purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: price (i.e., based on property appreciation) and then is assessed only on the amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appreciation in value (flip tax is profit-based); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount of the flip tax is less than or equal to 5% of the value of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest (calculated as the lesser of appraised value or sales price of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest) and is a flat fee, fee per share, percentage of the appraised value or sales price of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Interest, or dollar amount per room
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Cooperative Share Loan lien priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Cooperative Share Loan must be a lien that has priority over all other claims or liens
elements.label: text

elements.text: against the Shareholder’s Cooperative Shares in the Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The lien may be subordinated to the Cooperative Corporation’s lien against the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Shares for unpaid assessments that represent the Pro Rata Share of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation’s payments for the Blanket Mortgage and/or the current year’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate taxes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Cooperative Projects located in New York, a Cooperative Share Loan may
elements.label: text

elements.text: additionally be subordinated to unpaid Maintenance Fees and assessments accrued after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the origination date of the Cooperative Share Loan and collection expenses
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must also ensure that all the following requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Shareholder has the right to encumber his or her Cooperative Interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Shareholder’s right to occupy the Cooperative Unit pursuant to the Proprietary Lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: extends through the Maturity Date of the Cooperative Share Loan either by its term or
elements.label: text

elements.text: through renewals
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Cooperative Share Loan is secured by a pledge or trust of the Cooperative Shares,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the assignment of interest in the Proprietary Lease and any other documents appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: under individual State or local laws, ordinances and practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Cooperative Share Loan is a valid, enforceable first lien on the Shareholder’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Interest in the Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. All UCC-1 and renewal statements as applicable are current and valid. In States where
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Units are considered real property, perfection of the lien must comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable State or local laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The Seller has obtained the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An assignment of the Proprietary Lease for each Cooperative Share Loan sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The stock power or other equivalent document authorizing the Seller/Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer Cooperative Shares in the event of a default; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Valid financing statements and assignments of financing statements (executed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed if necessary) to perfect Freddie Mac’s security interest under the Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Commercial Code of the State in which the Cooperative Unit is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Cooperative Corporation’s approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants that if the purchaser’s right to a Cooperative Share or
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupancy of a Cooperative Unit is subject to any right of the Cooperative Corporation to
elements.label: text

elements.text: give approval, then the Seller is required to furnish evidence to clearly show that such
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval has been given before the Cooperative Share Loan is eligible for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase or securitization
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Cooperative Project that is not a Cooperative Housing Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Cooperative Share Loan to be eligible for Freddie Mac purchase or securitization, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project must meet Internal Revenue Service (IRS) requirements (Section 216)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a Cooperative Housing Corporation in effect as of the Delivery Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shareholders in a Cooperative Housing Corporation can claim their Pro Rata Share of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage interest and real-property taxes as income tax deductions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.6: Legal requirements for New Cooperative
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Projects (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Amendments to Cooperative Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rights of Cooperative Shareholders and Sellers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative membership requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Corporation’s lien position
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assignment of Shareholder’s Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Corporation responsibilities and lender rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Cooperative Share Loans delivered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 5705.7 for Exempt from Review, when a Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivers a Cooperative Share Loan secured by a Cooperative Interest in a New Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project to Freddie Mac, the Seller represents and warrants all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Compliance with law
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Corporation is a validly formed Entity authorized to carry out its
elements.label: text

elements.text: independent purposes and is compliant with all applicable State and local laws and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordinances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Cooperative Corporation has retained the right to provide a substitute purchaser, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right to have the first option to purchase a Cooperative Unit, or the right to approve a
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchaser, those rights will not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be exercised in a way that could be interpreted as discrimination; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Impair the marketability of the Cooperative Units in the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Amendments to Cooperative Project Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Shareholders have the right to amend the Cooperative Project Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Rights of Cooperative Shareholders and Sellers
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Corporation is legally bound to notify the Seller of any material adverse
elements.label: text

elements.text: changes including, but not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Threatened or actual condemnation, eminent domain proceeding or acquisition or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: actual loss, whether covered by insurance, that affects any portion of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project or Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The inability of Shareholders to deduct their Pro Rata Share of Mortgage interest from
elements.label: text

elements.text: their taxable income per the Internal Revenue Code (i.e., the Cooperative Corporation no
elements.label: text

elements.text: longer qualifies as a Cooperative Housing Corporation pursuant to Section 216 of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Internal Revenue Code)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Any 30-day Delinquency by the Cooperative Corporation in payments due under any
elements.label: text

elements.text: underlying Blanket Mortgage, whether the payments are for real estate taxes, assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: or charges imposed by a government entity or public utility or made under the terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any ground lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Any lapse, cancelation or material modification of any insurance or fidelity insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverage maintained by the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Any 90-day Delinquency by the Shareholder that is related to the payment of his or her
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees or other assessments; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Any proposed action that requires the consent of a specified percentage of eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: lenders of Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Cooperative membership requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project Documents must meet all the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Require that the sale or transfer of Cooperative Shares, stock or membership certificates
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Cooperative Corporation comply with federal and State security disclosure laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Require the Shareholders to own Shares, stock or a membership certificate and permit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Shares, stock or membership certificates to be pledged and registered in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Give the Shareholder a right to occupy the Cooperative Unit for a period that extends at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least to the maturity date of the Cooperative Share Loan in accordance with the terms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions of the Proprietary Lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Prohibit the Cooperative Corporation from imposing unreasonable limitations on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shareholder’s ability to sell, transfer or convey his or her Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Cooperative Corporation’s lien position
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Corporation has a good and marketable title to the Cooperative Project land,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Units and Common Elements, and the Cooperative Project is free and clear of
elements.label: text

elements.text: liens and encumbrances except the underlying Blanket Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Assignment of Shareholder’s Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project Documents and the Recognition Agreement must not permit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation to restrict the sale, conveyance or transfer of a Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned by a lender, its successors or assigns or place any limits on the assignment of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Proprietary Lease to the lender, its successors or assigns. The Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents and the Recognition Agreement must also permit the lender, its successors or
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigns to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer the Cooperative Shares and Proprietary Lease if the Shareholder defaults on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Select a non-corporate designee for any transfer of the Cooperative Shares and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Proprietary Lease in the event of a foreclosure if the Cooperative Project Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: require that a Shareholder be a natural person
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Cooperative Corporation responsibilities and lender rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project Documents or Recognition Agreement must include provisions to
elements.label: text
