elements.text: recognize specific rights of the lender that finances a Cooperative Share Loan and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation’s responsibilities to that lender, its successors or assigns. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project Documents must provide for at least the following corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities and lender rights:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Corporation responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project Documents, Recognition Agreement or other legal documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: include, but are not limited to, the following responsibilities of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) In the event of Shareholder’s default on the Cooperative Share Loan and at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: request of the lender of the Cooperative Share Loan, the Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evict the Shareholder; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terminate the Shareholder’s Proprietary Lease at the lender’s expense
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) In the event of the Shareholder’s default on the Cooperative Share Loan, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Corporation must also issue Cooperative Share(s) and the Proprietary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Lease in the name of the lender or its designee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Lender rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project Documents or Recognition Agreement must grant the lender of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan the right to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Cure the Shareholder’s default in payment of Maintenance Fees or other assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Review and approve the following actions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any surrender, cancelation, modification, assignment or pledge of any documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidencing ownership, possession and use of the Shareholder’s Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any further or additional pledge or Mortgage of any documents evidencing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership, possession and use of the Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any action to change the size, existence or form of ownership of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.7: Exempt From Review (09/30/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General Cooperative Project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible under Exempt From Review, the requirements below must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Cooperative Share Loan; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Refi Possible® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Cooperative Project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be a cooperative hotel, houseboat project, timeshare project or project with
elements.label: text

elements.text: segmented ownership (all as described in Section 5705.3)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be in need of Critical Repairs (see Section 5705.3(q) for details)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not have an evacuation order (see Section 5705.3(r) for details); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be the subject of an action that would cause the Cooperative Project to cease to exist
elements.label: text

elements.text: and not be the subject of an insolvency proceeding (see Section 5705.3(s) for details)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) General Cooperative Project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The general Cooperative Project eligibility requirements in Section 5705.2(b) must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following table describes additional requirements that must be met based on the type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional requirements by Mortgage type
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage type Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Cooperative Share Loan being
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinanced is currently owned by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in whole or in part or securitized by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, then the Mortgage is exempt
elements.label: text

elements.text: from project review provided the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum loan-to-value
elements.label: text

elements.text: (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Line of Credit (HELOC) TLTV
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HTLTV) ratio is 80%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If available, proof of the ULDD Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Point Related Investor Loan Identifier
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the existing Cooperative Share
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan is provided in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The delivery requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6302.45 are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refi Possible Mortgage If the Cooperative Share Loan being
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinanced is currently owned by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in whole or in part or securitized by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Mortgage is being
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinanced under the Refi Possible offering,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the Mortgage is exempt from project
elements.label: text

elements.text: review provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage meets all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Refi Possible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in Chapter 4302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The delivery requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6302.46 are met

elements.text: 5705.8: Appraisal requirements for Cooperative Units
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General appraisal requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comparable sale requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain an appraisal of the Cooperative Unit with an interior and exterior property
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection reported on Fannie Mae Form 2090, Individual Cooperative Interest Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report, which must be submitted to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® (UDCP®) in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Chapter 5606 and must receive a “Successful” status before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Delivery Date of the Cooperative Share Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Interest is the Cooperative Shares (or ownership interest) in the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation and the related occupancy rights, excluding the Cooperative Interest’s Pro Rata
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share of the underlying Blanket Mortgage. The Cooperative Interest is the equity portion that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is over and above the Pro Rata Share of the Blanket Mortgage(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General appraisal requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: When appraising a Cooperative Unit, appraisers must develop the opinion of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the Cooperative Interest. To determine the value of the Cooperative Interest, appraisers
elements.label: text

elements.text: must report and analyze including, but not limited to, the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Number of shares attributable to the subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Number of shares issued and outstanding for the Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lienholder name, lien position and the amount and repayment terms for all the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project’s blanket financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pro Rata Share of the Blanket Mortgage payments attributable to the subject unit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by dividing the number of shares attributable to the subject unit by the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of project shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pro Rata Share of each lien attributable to the subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any tax abatements or exemptions that are attributable to the subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remaining term for any tax abatements or exemptions and provisions for escalation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate taxes (dollar amount by which the taxes will increase and the year in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: increase occurs); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly Maintenance Fees, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Utility charges (if included in the fee)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Ground rent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Other fees for the use of the facilities that are attributable to the unit (fee type, amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: and term)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This information can generally be developed through Fannie Mae Form 1074, Request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project Information, if the management agent, Cooperative Corporation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: project sponsor/developer uses the form to respond to Seller or appraiser inquiries for project
elements.label: text

elements.text: information. When Fannie Mae Form 1074 is used, appraisers may either report the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate information in the appraisal report form or attach the Fannie Mae Form 1074 to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the appraisal report as an addendum.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting the information applicable to the Cooperative Unit and Cooperative Project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use reliable sources to obtain data on the Cooperative Project, the individual subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the comparable properties and indicate each source by name on the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: or an addendum to the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Address any factors that could result in an increase to the monthly debt service for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indicate the dollar amount of the monthly Maintenance Fees for each of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales in the Sales Comparison Approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report the value of the Cooperative Interest, excluding the Pro Rata Share of the Blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s). This value reflects the market value of the Cooperative Interest for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject unit (e.g., if the indicated value of the unit encumbered by the Blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s) is $100,000, and the unit’s Pro Rata Share of the Blanket Mortgage(s) is
elements.label: text

elements.text: $25,000, then the market value estimate that the appraiser must report for the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a certification in the appraisal report that the Pro Rata Share of the Blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s) on the real estate has not been included in the opinion of the market value of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Comparable sale requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisers must comment on the acceptance of housing cooperatives in the Market Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The degree of acceptance is generally reflected in the availability of similar comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: data for Cooperative Units. If there is limited market acceptance of the cooperative form of
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership or if cooperative forms of ownership are relatively new in the Market Area,
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisers must address any effect that has on the value and marketability of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit that is being appraised. The appraiser must compare the subject unit to the general
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area as well as to other units in the subject Cooperative Project to demonstrate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: market acceptance of Cooperative Units in the area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General comparable sale requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales must be from similar types of projects that have similar Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements and recreational facilities, including, but not limited to, townhouses and mid￾rise and high-rise buildings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisers must use Cooperative Units as comparable sales when they are available.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisers may also use condominium units as comparable sales if Cooperative Units are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not available, with an explanation for why those types of comparables were used. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser must adjust the condominium comparables to reflect the reaction of the market
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Cooperative Unit when there is a preference for condominium ownership in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject Market Area. (See Section 5605.6(g) for general requirements regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales selection.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable sale requirements for units in Established Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Cooperative Units located in Established Cooperative Projects, the appraiser should
elements.label: text

elements.text: use comparable sales from within the Cooperative Project when they are the best
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicators of value for the subject property. The use of comparable sales that are located
elements.label: text

elements.text: outside of the established subject Cooperative Project must be explained in the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the subject Cooperative Unit is in an Established Cooperative Project, appraisers
elements.label: text

elements.text: should use the following as comparable sales:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Two comparable sales from within the subject Cooperative Project, when available;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One comparable sale from a competing Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5605.6(g) for general requirements regarding selection of comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Comparable sale requirements for units in recently converted or New Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject Cooperative Unit is in a recently converted or New Cooperative Project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisers should use the following as comparable sales:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One comparable sale from the subject Cooperative Project, when available; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Two comparable sales from outside of the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the subject project is so new that a closed (settled) sale is not available,
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales from competing projects must be used. The appraiser must comment on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the marketability of the new project and justify and support the use of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales from outside the Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5605.6(g) for general requirements regarding comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: selection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.8: Appraisal requirements for Cooperative Units
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General appraisal requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comparable sale requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain a Traditional Appraisal Report of the Cooperative Unit which must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® (UDCP®) in accordance with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Chapter 5606 and must receive a “Successful” status before the Delivery Date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Interest is the Cooperative Shares (or ownership interest) in the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation and the related occupancy rights, excluding the Cooperative Interest’s Pro Rata
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share of the underlying Blanket Mortgage. The Cooperative Interest is the equity portion that
elements.label: text

elements.text: is over and above the Pro Rata Share of the Blanket Mortgage(s).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) General appraisal requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The opinion of market value for a Cooperative Unit must be based on the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest. The appraisal report must include information and additional commentary as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable including, but not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Number of shares attributable to the subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Number of shares issued and outstanding for the Cooperative Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lien position and the amount and repayment terms for all the Cooperative Project’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: blanket financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pro Rata Share of the Blanket Mortgage payments attributable to the subject unit,
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by dividing the number of shares attributable to the subject unit by the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of project shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pro Rata Share of each lien attributable to the subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any tax abatements or exemptions that are attributable to the subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remaining term for any tax abatements or exemptions and provisions for escalation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate taxes (dollar amount by which the taxes will increase and the year in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: increase occurs); and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monthly Maintenance Fees, including:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Utility charges (if included in the fee)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Ground rent; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Other fees for the use of the facilities that are attributable to the unit (fee type, amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: and term)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This information can generally be developed through Fannie Mae Form 1074, Request for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project Information, if the management agent, Cooperative Corporation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: project sponsor/developer uses the form to respond to Seller or appraiser inquiries for project
elements.label: text

elements.text: information. The appraisal report must include the appropriate information in the “Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information” and the “Cooperative Information” sections of the appraisal report. Fannie Mae
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 1074 may be included as an exhibit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When reporting the information applicable to the Cooperative Unit and Cooperative Project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use reliable sources to obtain data on the Cooperative Project, the individual subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the comparable properties and indicate each source by name on the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Address any factors that could result in an increase to the monthly debt service for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indicate the dollar amount of the monthly Maintenance Fees for each of the comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales in the Sales Comparison Approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Report the value of the Cooperative Interest, excluding the Pro Rata Share of the Blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s). This value reflects the market value of the Cooperative Interest for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject unit (e.g., if the indicated value of the unit encumbered by the Blanket
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage(s) is $100,000, and the unit’s Pro Rata Share of the Blanket Mortgage(s) is
elements.label: text

elements.text: $25,000, then the market value estimate that must be reported for the Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Comparable sale requirements for units in Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include comments on the acceptance of housing cooperatives in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area. The degree of acceptance is generally reflected in the availability of similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales data for Cooperative Units. If there is limited market acceptance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: cooperative form of ownership or if cooperative forms of ownership are relatively new in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Market Area, the appraisal report must address any effect that has on the value and
elements.label: text

elements.text: marketability of the Cooperative Unit that is being appraised. The subject unit must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: compared to the general Market Area as well as to other units in the subject Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project to demonstrate the market acceptance of Cooperative Units in the area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General comparable sale requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales must be from similar types of projects that have similar Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements and recreational facilities, including, but not limited to, townhouses and mid￾rise and high-rise buildings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must use Cooperative Units as comparable sales when they are
elements.label: text

elements.text: available. Condominium units may be used as comparable sales if Cooperative Units are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not available and the appraisal report must include an explanation for why those types of
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparables were used. Adjustments must be made to the condominium comparables to
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflect the reaction of the market to the Cooperative Unit when there is a preference for
elements.label: text

elements.text: condominium ownership in the subject Market Area. (See Section 5605.7(g) for general
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements regarding comparable sales selection.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable sale requirements for units in Established Cooperative Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Cooperative Units located in Established Cooperative Projects, comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: from within the Cooperative Project should be used when they are the best indicators of
elements.label: text

elements.text: value for the subject property. The use of comparable sales that are located outside of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: established subject Cooperative Project must be explained in the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the subject Cooperative Unit is in an Established Cooperative Project, the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report should include the following as comparable sales:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Two comparable sales from within the subject Cooperative Project, when available;
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One comparable sale from a competing Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5605.7(f) for general requirements regarding selection of comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: sales.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Comparable sale requirements for units in recently converted or New Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject Cooperative Unit is in a recently converted or New Cooperative Project, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report should include the following as comparable sales:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ One comparable sale from the subject Cooperative Project, when available; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Two comparable sales from outside of the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the subject project is so new that a closed (settled) sale is not available,
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sales from competing projects must be used. The appraisal report must
elements.label: text

elements.text: include commentary on the marketability of the new project and justification and support
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the use of comparable sales from outside the Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 5605.7(f) for general requirements regarding comparable sales
elements.label: text

elements.text: selection.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.9: Combined Cooperative Units (03/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum number of units after Cooperative Units are combined
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements to combine Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan purpose for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal requirements for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Closing requirements for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing/foreclosure requirements for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Units that have been physically and legally combined to create a single Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unit must meet the following requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Minimum number of units after Cooperative Units are combined
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Project must be composed of at least two Cooperative Units after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: combination of any units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Requirements to combine Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prior to sale of the Cooperative Share Loan to Freddie Mac, the following must occur:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Cooperative Corporation must approve the combination of two or more adjoining
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Units into a single Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The construction to combine the Cooperative Units into a single Cooperative Unit must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Cooperative Corporation must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assign a single unit designation (i.e., a mailing address) to the combined single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Designate the number of Cooperative Shares applicable to the combined single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adjust the Maintenance Fees based on the total number of Cooperative Shares
elements.label: text

elements.text: assigned to the combined single Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Loan purpose for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Share Loan may be either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A purchase transaction for the two or more adjoining Cooperative Units on the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A refinance transaction, if a Shareholder owns one Cooperative Unit and is purchasing
elements.label: text

elements.text: one or more additional adjoining Cooperative Units. The proceeds from the refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loan may only be used to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Purchase the additional adjoining units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Pay off the current Cooperative Share Loan on the Cooperative Unit in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shareholder has a Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Pay the costs of construction to combine the Cooperative Units into a single unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Pay any related Closing Costs, financing costs and Prepaid/Escrows up to 2.5% of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UPB of the refinance loan amount; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Disburse cash to the Shareholder up to 1% of the UPB of the refinance Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share Loan or $2,000 whichever is less, provided that the cash disbursed does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed 5% of the amount of the Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Appraisal requirements for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Units must be appraised as a combined single unit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Closing requirements for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with the closing of the Cooperative Share Loan secured by the combined single
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Unit, the following requirements must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All loan documents must use the single unit designation assigned by the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Cooperative Share Loan must be secured by a first lien on the Cooperative Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., rights of ownership and occupancy rights) to the Cooperative Units that were
elements.label: text

elements.text: combined into the single unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Servicing/foreclosure requirements for combined Cooperative Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Cooperative Share Loan must be serviced as a 1-unit dwelling. In the event of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure, the Cooperative Share Loan secured by a pledge or trust of the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Shares must be foreclosed as a combined single 1-unit dwelling.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.10: Cooperative Project conversions (03/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project conversions with prior use
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Project conversion documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prior Cooperative Project financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Cooperative Project that was created by the conversion of a building(s) with a prior use, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements must be met for Seller’s review and determination of Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility in addition to all other applicable requirements for Cooperative Projects in the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cooperative Project conversions with prior use
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Non-Gut Rehabilitation conversion
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conversions involving a Non-Gut Rehabilitation of a Cooperative Project that was
elements.label: text

elements.text: legally created within three years of underwriting the Cooperative Project must meet all
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. An engineer’s report must confirm that the Cooperative Project is structurally sound
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The condition and remaining useful life of the major Cooperative Project components
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., the roof, elevators and mechanical systems, such as central heating, ventilation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and air conditioning (HVAC); plumbing and electricity) are sufficient to meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential needs of the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. All rehabilitation work involved in the conversion was completed in a professional
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There is no evidence of any adverse conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Gut Rehabilitation and Non-Gut Rehabilitation conversions over three years old
elements.label: text

elements.text: A review of the engineer’s report is not required for conversions involving a Gut
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rehabilitation or Non-Gut Rehabilitation if more than three years have elapsed since the
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal creation of the Cooperative Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Cooperative Project conversion documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Cooperative Project that was created by the conversion of a building(s) with a prior use
elements.label: text

elements.text: within three years of underwriting the Cooperative Project, the Seller’s project review and
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination of project eligibility must include a review of all the Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For an existing Cooperative Project and Cooperative Project conversions if more than three
elements.label: text

elements.text: years have elapsed since the legal creation of the Cooperative Project, a review of all the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project Documents is not required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Prior Cooperative Project financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: In a conversion, the mortgagee of the Blanket Mortgage agrees to the use of the building as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project, and, in the event of a default on the Blanket Mortgage, it will not wipe
elements.label: text

elements.text: out the Cooperative Shares of the Shareholders who are current in the payment of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maintenance Fees or assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.11: Documents to be delivered to the Document
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Custodian and other documents to be maintained by
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retention of Cooperative Project documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document Custodian documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgage file documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A copy of the Recognition Agreement and a copy of the Proprietary Lease must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintained in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Other file documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller must maintain the following documentation on the Cooperative Corporation and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Project:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Most recent two year’s financial statements including income and expense statement and
elements.label: text

elements.text: all footnotes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Current fiscal year’s operating budget
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. One of the following forms of evidence that the Cooperative Project meets insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Original policy (including a commercial package policy under which the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: coverages may be provided in whole or in part) and applicable endorsements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Copy of the original policy and applicable endorsements, if the copy meets the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file retention requirements of Chapter 3302; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Certificate, evidence or declarations of insurance, which must include the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name of insured Cooperative Corporation (as “First Named Insured”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name and address of Cooperative Project mortgagee(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Address of insured Cooperative Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Type, limit and effective dates of coverage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deductible amount and applicable coverage for each deductible
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any endorsement or optional coverage obtained and made part of the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurer’s agreement to provide at least 10 days’ notice to the Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation, the Cooperative Project mortgagee and the Cooperative Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee before any reduction in coverage or cancelation of the policy; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Signature of an authorized representative of the insurer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain a specimen of each policy and endorsement for which a
elements.label: text

elements.text: certificate, evidence or declarations of insurance is maintained in lieu of the policy and
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Retention of Cooperative Project documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must retain all Cooperative Project documentation that supports its
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranty that the Cooperative Project meets Freddie Mac requirements for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As long as Freddie Mac retains an interest in the applicable Cooperative Share Loan, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least seven years from the date Freddie Mac’s interest in the Cooperative Share Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: is satisfied
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Document Custodian documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6304.2(b) for documents that Seller/Servicers must submit to the Document
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodian.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5705.12: Pooling and delivery requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans (03/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6202.3(e)(vii) for information on pooling and delivery requirements for fixed-rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Share Loans under the fixed-rate Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.45 for special delivery requirements for Cooperative Share Loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: There are no special pooling or delivery requirements for adjustable-rate Cooperative
elements.label: text

elements.text: Share Loans.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5706: Manufactured Homes on
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Leasehold Estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5706.1: Purchase of Mortgages secured by
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Manufactured Homes on leasehold estates (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase a Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home on a leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: when there is demonstrated market acceptance. Mortgages that are secured by a Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Home on a leasehold estate must meet the requirements of this Chapter 5706.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval before selling Mortgages secured by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home on a leasehold estate to Freddie Mac by contacting its Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: representative or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Manufactured Homes on leasehold estates must satisfy the provisions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5703 and related Guide sections pertaining to Manufactured Homes and Chapter 5704
elements.label: text

elements.text: and related Guide sections pertaining to leasehold estates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5706.2: Property eligibility for Mortgages secured by
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Manufactured Homes on leasehold estates (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for purchase by Freddie Mac, a Manufactured Home on a leasehold estate must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A one-unit dwelling comprised of multiple sections (a “multiwide Manufactured Home”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Located on a leasehold estate meeting the requirements of Chapter 5704; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Located in a ground lease community
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For ground lease communities that are Condominium Projects, the Seller must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the Condominium Project requirements and warranties in Chapter 5701
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For ground lease communities that are Planned Unit Developments (PUDs), the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the PUD requirements and warranties in Chapter 5702
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Manufactured Home on a leasehold estate must not include an ADU.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Chapter 4504 for requirements for HeritageOne® Mortgages, including the special
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for HeritageOne Mortgages that are leasehold Mortgages secured by Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homes in Section 4504.8.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5706.3: Eligible transaction types for Mortgages
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: secured by Manufactured Homes on leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ “No cash-out” refinance transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Mortgage secured by a Manufactured Home located on a leasehold estate may be a purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction or a “no cash-out” refinance transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a new Manufactured Home, whether it is affixed to a permanent foundation prior to or after
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Application Received Date, the Seller must obtain a copy of the manufacturer’s invoice and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home Purchase Agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Purchase transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A purchase transaction is one in which the Mortgage proceeds are used to finance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase of the Manufactured Home. The proceeds may also be used to purchase the
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold interest in the land, as the Borrower does not separately own the land.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Manufactured Homes, the following requirements apply:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purchase price may include documented costs for delivery and setup, installation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: permanent utility connections, including well and/or septic systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credits for wheels and axles and any Manufactured Home retailer rebates must be

elements.text: allowed for purchase transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The maximum loan-to-value (LTV)/total LTV (TLTV)/Home Equity Line of Credit

elements.text: (b) “No cash-out” refinance transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: A “no cash-out” refinance transaction involves the payoff of an existing Mortgage secured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home and leasehold interest in the land. Proceeds from a “no cash-out”
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation plan, for the existing first Mortgage secured by the Manufactured Home and
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold interest in the land
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off any costs or fees associated with the satisfaction and release of the first Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., late fees, prepayment penalties, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay off or pay down any junior lien(s) obtained by the Borrower solely to acquire the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home. Any remaining balance must be subordinated to the refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pay related Closing Costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disburse cash out to the Borrower (or any other payee) up to the greater of 1% of the new
elements.label: text

elements.text: refinance Mortgage or $2,000
elements.label: text

elements.text: A “no cash-out” refinance Mortgage must also meet the requirements in Section 4301.4(a).

elements.text: (a) Lease provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements in Section 5704.1(c), the lease must also provide that
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Home and the leasehold estate.

elements.label: text

elements.text: The provisions of Section 5703.8(b) pertaining to calculating the treatment of the land in
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining value for computing the loan-to-value ratio in different types of financing
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions structures do not apply to leasehold estates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The provisions of Section 5703.7(b)(i) pertaining to the use of the land as an equity
elements.label: text

elements.text: contribution do not apply to the leasehold estate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The provisions of Section 5703.9 pertaining to the appraisal requirements for Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homes do apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Although Section 5704.2 pertaining to appraisal requirements for leasehold Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: allows for the use of comparable sales that are not on a leasehold estate, at least two
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable properties that are also on a leasehold estate must be included in the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report to demonstrate market acceptance.
