elements.text: 5706.6: Title issues and lien requirements relating to
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Manufactured Homes on leasehold estates (06/04/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ State permits surrender of certificate of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) State permits surrender of certificate of title
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Manufactured Home on a leasehold estate must be located in a State that permits surrender
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a certificate of title, regardless of whether the land on which the Manufactured Home is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located is owned by the owner of the Manufactured Housing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Title insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements in Section 5703.4(e), the American Land Title Association
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ALTA) Form 13.1 endorsement must be attached to the title insurance policy. The title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance policy must include the Manufactured Home as part of the lessee’s leasehold
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements and must include the value of the lessee’s leasehold improvements, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Manufactured Home, in the insured estate. All requirements necessary to obtain title
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance, including ALTA Form 13.1 and Form 7 endorsements, must be satisfied,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including any requirements necessary to ensure the leasehold transaction, such as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recordation of the lease or a short form lease that incorporates the various provisions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unrecorded lease.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An attorney opinion of title letter is not acceptable in lieu of a title insurance policy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 5801: The Uniform Closing Dataset
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 5801.1: The Uniform Closing Dataset and Loan
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Closing Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information and requirements relating to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Uniform Closing Dataset’s (UCD) function in electronic data transmission to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UCD
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ UCD messaging through Loan Closing Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) The UCD’s function in electronic data transmission to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UCD is a common industry dataset that allows information on the Settlement/Closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosure Statement to be communicated electronically.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The specific active version of the UCD Specification provides field-specific standardization
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for preparing the UCD in proper XML format for file transmission to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Closing Advisor is Freddie Mac’s electronic collection solution for the UCD that helps
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers validate that their closing data aligns with the UCD. Loan Closing Advisor assesses
elements.label: text

elements.text: the data against the specific active version of the UCD Specification, checking for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: completeness, validity and accuracy of certain calculated values, as well as consistency of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) The UCD
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements apply to the UCD for Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The UCD XML file with the embedded Settlement/Closing Disclosure Statement (PDF)
elements.label: text

elements.text: is required on all Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac with a Note Date on or after September
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The requirement to submit the UCD through Loan Closing Advisor is satisfied when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction has received data quality feedback messages and the final Settlement/Closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disclosure Statement PDF is embedded in the UCD XML file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The specific active version of the UCD Specification may be amended from time to time.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The current version can be found at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/uniform￾mortgage-data-program/ucd.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) UCD messaging through Loan Closing Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the UCD XML file is submitted to Loan Closing Advisor, the Seller will receive a
elements.label: text

elements.text: variety of feedback messages designed to assist the Seller in evaluating the closing data
elements.label: text

elements.text: against the UCD delivery specification requirements and determining if the associated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage meets the requirements of the specific active version of the UCD Specification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including those related to the completeness, validity and accuracy of certain calculated values
elements.label: text

elements.text: and consistency of the data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The presence of one or more feedback message(s) with a warning severity indicator at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of delivery does not prevent a submission from satisfying the UCD submission
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6101: Fixed-Rate Cash
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 6101.1: Fixed-rate Cash eligibility (11/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Sellers for fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages under fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Sellers for fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac under fixed-rate Cash, the Seller must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: entered into Purchase Contracts with Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Margin requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, at its sole discretion, require Sellers to post margin in connection with
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions under this Chapter 6101. In all events, Freddie Mac shall be the sole
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation agent with respect to the required margin amount and whether the margin
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount is to be posted or returned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Margin deficit notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac provides notice of a margin deficit by 10:00 a.m. Eastern time on any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day, the Seller shall transfer any required margin no later than the close of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business on such Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If such notice is received after 10:00 a.m. Eastern time on any Business Day or on a day
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is not a Business Day, the Seller shall transfer such margin no later than the close of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business on the next Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Establishing a Securities trading account
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller may establish a trading account with Freddie Mac that allows the Seller to
elements.label: text

elements.text: engage in the purchase and sale of UMBS® and Freddie Mac Mortgage Participation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To request approval to establish such an account, Sellers may e-mail or call the Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Window at Cashdesk_Traders@FreddieMac.com, or (571) 382-3080.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any securities trading activities are subject to the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sellers must comply with the margining terms and credit limits set forth in Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 102, Securities Trading Customer Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will be the calculation agent for purposes of determining any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts payable pursuant to the terms of any margin provisions, and such
elements.label: text

elements.text: determinations by Freddie Mac shall be final and binding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages under fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligible Mortgages under fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase fixed-rate Mortgages under fixed-rate Cash.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Exhibit 17S, Available Mortgage Products, for more information regarding eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for fixed-rate Mortgages sold under fixed-rate Cash.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages eligible for sale through Cash-Released XChange® under a Mandatory Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract or a Best Efforts Contract can be found on Freddie Mac’s website at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/content/_assets/resources/pdf/fact￾sheet/available_products.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii)Eligible original terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Term requirements for fixed-rate Mortgages sold under fixed-rate Cash are exceptions to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 4201.3 and are listed in the table below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Term requirements for Mortgages sold under fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage type Term requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10-year Mortgages 85–120 months from the date that is one month before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date as described in Section 4201.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: 15-year Mortgages 121–180 months from the date that is one month before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date as described in Section 4201.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: 20-year Mortgages 181–240 months from the date that is one month before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date as described in Section 4201.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: 30-year Mortgages 241–360 months from the date that is one month before the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date as described in Section 4201.3
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6101.2: Fixed-rate Cash Contracts (11/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mandatory fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Best Efforts fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Certain negotiated pricing under Fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mandatory fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Offer amounts and procedures for Mandatory Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Business Day, the Seller may take out Mandatory Cash Contracts to sell one or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more Mortgages. Freddie Mac will endeavor to announce prior to the offer day if
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitments will not be available due to holidays or other reasons.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum amount for an individual Mandatory Cash Contract is $1,000. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may, at its discretion and at any time, impose a maximum contract amount for individual
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mandatory Cash Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may enter into multiple mandatory cash contracts but may not exceed $200
elements.label: text

elements.text: million in aggregate fixed-rate and WAC ARM (see Section 6102.2(a)) commitment
elements.label: text

elements.text: volume per day. The Seller may seek an exception to this amount by contacting the Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Desk (Cash_Ex@FreddieMac.com or 571-382-5960).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If this limit is exceeded without prior Freddie Mac approval, Freddie Mac may pair
elements.label: text

elements.text: off the Seller’s contract(s) or may require the Seller to pair off the contract(s) at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller obligations for Mandatory Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under a Mandatory Cash Contract, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell to Freddie Mac one or more Mortgages that meet all of the contract requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: and have an aggregate UPB equal to the contract amount, subject to the purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: tolerance applicable to cash contracts under Section 6401.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Complete all requirements for the sale of the Mortgages by the close of business on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Contract Expiration Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the Purchase Contract are provided in Loan Selling Advisor® and made
elements.label: text

elements.text: available in Loan Selling Advisor’s Purchase Contract Confirmation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Contract periods for Mandatory Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Loan Selling Advisor, the Purchase Contract Effective Period is the period of time for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which a price for eligible Mortgages provided to the Seller by Loan Selling Advisor is
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective. The Seller may select a Purchase Contract Effective Period of between two and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 90 days or such other time frame as permitted by Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All of the requirements for all Mortgages under the contract must be completed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Contract Expiration Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Pricing for Mandatory Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pricing is available in Loan Selling Advisor each Business Day and is based on prices
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects to receive in the securities market for the related UMBS® or MBS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The price also reflects the Mortgage Note Rate, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the delivery period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will update prices on active Mortgage coupons throughout the day to reflect
elements.label: text

elements.text: movements in the securities market. Posted prices assume a 0.250% Minimum Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spread and the use of the Standard Remittance Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The contract price the Seller receives through Loan Selling Advisor may be adjusted as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller allocates Mortgages to the contract or provides more detailed Mortgage data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller indemnifies Freddie Mac for any and all costs and expenses incurred by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as a result of the Seller’s sale of a Mortgage that Freddie Mac priced and
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased under a Mandatory Cash Contract based on incomplete and/or inaccurate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage data provided by the Seller for which Freddie Mac would have provided a
elements.label: text

elements.text: different price if the Mortgage data provided by the Seller had been complete and
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Once the Seller has accepted and agreed to the contract price provided by Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is a binding commitment by Freddie Mac to purchase, and by the Seller to sell,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages under the contract terms (subject to the conformity of each Mortgage to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Purchase Documents).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Specified payups for Mandatory Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with certain attributes, the Seller may be eligible to receive specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: payups to standard cash pricing. The contract must be a 10-, 15-, 20- or 30-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: mandatory fixed-rate contract. The cash payups are eligible for full commitment periods
elements.label: text

elements.text: including extensions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Mortgages within the contract must meet the requirements of the payup. Otherwise,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller will not be able to allocate the Mortgage to the contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage characteristics that are eligible for specified payups are available in Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Super conforming Mortgages sold under 100% super conforming Cash Contracts may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be subject to price adjustments. See Chapter 4603 for special eligibility requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: super conforming Mortgages. See Section 6302.31 for delivery requirements for fixed￾rate Cash Contracts for super conforming Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For more details: go to https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/selling￾delivery/delivery-options-pricing/cash-payups.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Pairoff process for Mandatory Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the contract amount will not be fulfilled by delivery of Mortgages, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: may request a pairoff via Loan Selling Advisor or may wait until the contract expires and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor automatically calculates the pairoff fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The pairoff process used in Loan Selling Advisor is “two-way” and may result in either a
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee due Freddie Mac from the Seller or a fee due the Seller from Freddie Mac, depending
elements.label: text

elements.text: on market conditions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may also choose to allocate Mortgages to “pair off and transfer to a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract” (or pair off and recommit). With this functionality, the Seller may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pair off part or all of a current contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take out a new contract with different terms and/or a different amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer non-funded loans to a new contract to extend the Purchase Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Effective Period or increase the Purchase Contract amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: A pairoff fee calculated and provided by Loan Selling Advisor is non-negotiable. Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac reserves the right to amend the provisions of this section or Section 6401.1 relating
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Loan Selling Advisor pairoff process without prior notice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii)Mandatory Cash Contract extensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the contract amount specified in a Mandatory Cash Contract will not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: fulfilled on or prior to the contract expiration date, the Seller may extend, in Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor, the Mandatory Cash Contract at any time on or prior to the contract expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may extend the contract an unlimited number of times, as long as the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension period plus the original contract term does not exceed 120 days and each
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension is less than 90 days beyond the current date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In exchange for the contract extension, the Seller will be assessed a fee to be paid in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 6303.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Best Efforts fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Offer amounts and procedures for Best Efforts Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Business Day, the Seller may take out a Best Effort Contract to sell a specific fixed￾rate Mortgage. Freddie Mac will endeavor to announce prior to the offer day if
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitments will not be available due to holidays or other reasons.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum amount for an individual Best Efforts Contract is $1,000. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may, at its discretion and at any time, impose a maximum contract amount for Best
elements.label: text

elements.text: Efforts Cash Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller obligations for Best Efforts Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under a Best Efforts Contract, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use its best efforts to close and fund a specified Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell the Mortgage to Freddie Mac, at a cash price determined by Freddie Mac, if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage with the same Borrower(s) and property address is closed and funded
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ With respect to Mortgages sold under a Best Efforts Contract, no later than close of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business on the Business Day following the day on which the Seller denies the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s Mortgage application or the Borrower exercises his/her right of
elements.label: text

elements.text: rescission, notify Freddie Mac of the change in the Mortgage application status or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: rescission, as applicable, via Loan Selling Advisor (also known as “Withdraw Loan”
elements.label: text

elements.text: status). Such notification must occur within the Purchase Contract Effective Period.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The terms of the Purchase Contract are provided in Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Contract periods for Best Efforts Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Loan Selling Advisor, the Purchase Contract Effective Period is the period of time for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which a price for eligible Mortgages provided to the Seller by Loan Selling Advisor is
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective. The Seller may select a Purchase Contract Effective Period of between two and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 90 days or such other time frame as permitted by Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All of the requirements for the specified Mortgage under the contract must be completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Purchase Contract Expiration Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage specified under a Best Efforts Contract closes and funds after the Best
elements.label: text

elements.text: Efforts Contract expires, the Seller remains obligated to sell the Mortgage to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: under that same Best Efforts Contract. See Section 6101.2(b)(iv) below for the impact on
elements.label: text

elements.text: pricing of selling a Mortgage under a Best Efforts Contract that has expired.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, despite the Seller’s best efforts, the Mortgage is not closed and funded, the Seller will
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be assessed a pairoff fee. The Seller may substitute a different loan to fulfill this
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract. Pricing adjustments may apply. See Section 6101.2(b)(iv) for Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts. However, the Seller indemnifies Freddie Mac for any and all costs and
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses incurred by Freddie Mac as a result of the Seller’s failure to sell a Mortgage to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac that is closed and funded by the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Furthermore, the Seller may not:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Substitute a Mortgage with a different Borrower(s) or a different property address for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage specified in the Best Efforts Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take out another Best Efforts Contract or a Mandatory Cash Contract, either
elements.label: text

elements.text: sequentially or at the same time, for the same Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Take out a Best Efforts Contract with fictitious Borrower data or a fictitious property
elements.label: text

elements.text: address
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell a Mortgage specified in a Best Efforts Contract to another investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller fails to comply with the requirements specified above, Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediately terminate the Seller’s eligibility to sell under a Best Efforts Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Pricing for Best Efforts Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pricing is available in Loan Selling Advisor each Business Day and is based on prices
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects to receive in the securities market for the related UMBS or MBS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The price also reflects the Mortgage Note Rate, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the delivery period and the cost that Freddie Mac incurs for managing interest rate risk on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will update prices on active Mortgage coupons throughout the day to reflect
elements.label: text

elements.text: movements in the securities market. Posted prices assume a 0.250% (25 basis point)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Contract Servicing Spread and the use of the Standard Remittance Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Among other considerations, Freddie Mac will consider the Seller’s success rate, under
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevailing market conditions, in closing Mortgages that are allocated to Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts. A Mortgage that fails to fund and close as specified in the Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract may adversely affect the Seller’s pricing for future Best Efforts Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages allocated to Best Efforts Contracts, if any of the characteristics change,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller must modify the Mortgage data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the Borrower switches from a 30-year fixed-rate Mortgage to a 15-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: fixed-rate Mortgage, the Mortgage data must be modified without allocating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to a new Best Efforts Contract. The contract will be re-priced using the prices
elements.label: text

elements.text: in effect as of the date that the Seller originally accepted the Best Efforts Contract price.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if the Seller sells a Mortgage that has closed and funded after a Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract has expired, Freddie Mac will determine the price for that Mortgage at the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: of sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller indemnifies Freddie Mac for any and all costs and expenses incurred by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac as a result of the Seller’s sale of a Mortgage that Freddie Mac priced and
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased under a Best Efforts Contract based on incomplete and/or inaccurate Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: data provided by the Seller, for which Freddie Mac would have provided a different price
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the Mortgage data provided by the Seller had been complete and accurate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Specified payups for Best Efforts Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with certain attributes, Sellers may be eligible to receive specified payups
elements.label: text

elements.text: to standard cash pricing. The contract must be a 10-, 15-, 20- or 30-year Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: fixed-rate contract. The cash payups are eligible for full commitment periods, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: extensions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Mortgages within the contract must meet the requirements of the payups. If a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage does not meet the requirements for the applicable Cash Contract, the Seller will
elements.label: text

elements.text: not be able to allocate the Mortgage to the contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage characteristics that are eligible for specified payups are available in Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Best Efforts Cash Contract extensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event the Mortgage specified in a Best Efforts Contract will not close prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract expiration date, the Seller may extend, in Loan Selling Advisor, the Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract at any time prior to the contract expiration date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may extend the contract an unlimited number of times, as long as the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension period plus the original contract term does not exceed 120 days and each
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension is less than 90 days beyond the current date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In exchange for the contract extension, the Seller will be assessed a fee, to be paid in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 6303.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Certain negotiated pricing under Fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, from time to time, assess certain negotiated Credit Fees. Any such pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be made available to the Seller on the cash Purchase Contract loan-level fee breakdown
elements.label: text

elements.text: screen in Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the provisions of Section 1101.2(a)(ii), Freddie Mac may amend, supplement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: revise or terminate any of the pricing or provisions implemented pursuant to this section in
elements.label: text

elements.text: whole or in part, upon written notice to the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6101.3: Purchase and settlement of fixed-rate Cash
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Contracts (11/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funding Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funding amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest paid to the Servicer in connection with Mortgage funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Funding Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: By the close of business on the Purchase Contract Expiration Date, the Seller must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed all the requirements for the sale of the Mortgages to Freddie Mac. Sellers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: request a specific Funding Date for each of the Mortgages sold under a Mandatory Cash or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Contract or may elect to have funding occur as soon as practicable after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for sale of the Mortgages to Freddie Mac have been met (“ASAP funding”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Funding amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: As each Mortgage funds, Freddie Mac will pay the Seller the amount calculated by multiplying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the price specified in the cash Purchase Contract by the UPB of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage is subject to Credit Fees or credits for Credit Fees, Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Net the fee or credit amounts against the amount paid to the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reflect any subsequent change to Credit Fees and credits for Credit Fees on the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly invoice
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Interest paid to the Servicer in connection with Mortgage funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with the Mortgage funding, Freddie Mac will determine the amount of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: to pay the Servicer on each Mortgage by computing the interest on the UPB of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the first day of the Funding Date month through the day before the Funding Date using
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Accounting Net Yield (ANY).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Thereafter, Freddie Mac will compensate the Servicer in an amount equal to the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread for each Mortgage and authorizes the Servicer to retain the Servicing Spread out of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payments made by the Borrower, and the Servicer will remit the ANY to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6101.4: Servicing options and remittance cycle for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: fixed-rate Cash Contracts (11/05/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing options for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance cycle for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum Contract Servicing Spread for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing options for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject to approval and conditions set by Freddie Mac, the Seller may sell fixed-rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages under Cash using one of the following Servicing options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing retained: The Seller sells the Mortgage to Freddie Mac and agrees to service
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgages in accordance with the Guide and applicable Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Concurrent Transfer of Servicing: The Seller sells the Mortgage to Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: agrees to service the Mortgages in accordance with the Guide and applicable Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents and concurrently engages in a Transfer of Servicing initiated by the Seller to
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Transferee Servicer with the Mortgage purchase and the Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurring on the Settlement Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer selected by the Seller must be a Freddie Mac-approved Servicer eligible to
elements.label: text

elements.text: service the particular Mortgage, and Freddie Mac must approve the Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Chapter 7101.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.51 for additional information regarding delivery requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold servicing-released under Mandatory Cash Contracts or Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-Released XChange®: The Seller sells the Mortgage servicing-released in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements of Section 6101.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Remittance cycle for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The remittance cycle that applies to Mortgages sold under a Mandatory Cash Contract or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Contract is the Standard Remittance Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The price that the Seller receives for the Mortgages under a Mandatory Cash Contract or Best
elements.label: text

elements.text: Efforts Contract will reflect the Standard Remittance Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Minimum Contract Servicing Spread for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicing Spread applicable to any individual Mortgage delivered pursuant to a specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Contract may be greater than or equal to, but not less than, the Minimum Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spread selected by the Seller for that specific Purchase Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread is selected by the Seller when taking out a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Contract and is applicable to all the Mortgages delivered pursuant to that specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Contract. The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be equal to or greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Minimum Servicing Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For fixed-rate Cash, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must be at least 0.250% and may not exceed 0.500% and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Must equal 0.250% for Mortgages sold through Cash-Released XChange
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with annual or monthly premium lender-paid mortgage insurance, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must select a Minimum Contract Servicing Spread that is sufficient to ensure that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer receives the Minimum Servicing Spread plus the amount necessary to pay the
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage insurance premium when due. Refer to Section 4701.2(b) for special eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Mortgages with lender-paid mortgage insurance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6101.5: Requirements for fixed-rate Cash Contracts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: through Cash-Released XChange®
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-Released XChange® overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cash-Released XChange pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Availability of servicing-released prices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funding adjustments and net funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identification of Transferee Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Excluding Transferee Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Authorization to disclose the Seller’s identity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer’s rights to screen out the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Late fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cash-Released XChange overview
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Mortgages are sold under a Mandatory Cash or Best Efforts Contract through Cash￾Released XChange®, Freddie Mac arranges for a Transferee Servicer, as identified by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, to accept a Concurrent Transfer of Servicing from the Seller at the time the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller sells the Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements applicable to the sale of Mortgages through Cash-Released XChange are
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in this chapter and Exhibit 28A, Loan Servicing Purchase and Sale Agreement for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-Released XChange®. Sellers approved to sell eMortgages may sell these Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Cash-Released XChange and must comply with the requirements of Chapter 1402
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller agrees to, and shall comply with, the terms of Exhibit 28A, which include, but are
elements.label: text

elements.text: not limited to, requirements for the Seller and for each Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The price the Seller receives for the Mortgages sold servicing- released reflects the Standard
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remittance Cycle. See Section 6101.4(c) for the applicable Minimum Contract Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller must apply to Freddie Mac for approval and access to participate in Cash-Released
elements.label: text

elements.text: XChange. If Freddie Mac determines that the Seller is eligible to participate in Cash￾Released XChange, Freddie Mac will approve the Seller’s use of and provide appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to Cash-Released XChange. The Seller must call their Freddie Mac representative or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 800-FREDDIE to inquire about the approval process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right, upon notice to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limit the aggregate UPB of all Mortgages that a Seller may sell to Freddie Mac through
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-Released Xchange
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Impose any additional requirements with respect to Cash-Released XChange, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suspend or terminate the Seller’s ability to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac through Cash￾Released XChange.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Cash-Released XChange pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The price provided by Freddie Mac to the Seller under a Mandatory Cash Contract or Best
elements.label: text

elements.text: Efforts Contract through Cash-Released XChange includes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The price offered by Freddie Mac for the purchase of the Mortgage(s), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The price offered by the Transferee Servicer for the Servicing Contract Rights related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Prices and fees provided when the Seller enters into a Mandatory Cash Contract or a Best
elements.label: text

elements.text: Efforts Contract through Cash-Released XChange are not final; instead, they are determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: as of the Funding Date and reflect the Mortgage data provided by the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The price will not include late fees that must be paid to Freddie Mac pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6101.5(i) below or to the Transferee Servicer as provided in Exhibit 28A. Such
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional late fees include fees for late delivery of documents to the Transferee Servicer and
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees for failure to transfer or reconcile funds in a timely manner.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Availability of servicing-released prices
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only fixed-rate Mortgages for which Loan Selling Advisor® provides a servicing-released
elements.label: text

elements.text: price may be sold servicing-released under a Mandatory Cash Contract or Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract. Not all Transferee Servicers will provide a bid for the assumption of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights for certain Mortgages. If no Transferee Servicer provides a bid for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption of Servicing Contract Rights for certain Mortgages, then Cash-Released
elements.label: text

elements.text: XChange is not available for these Mortgages, which may otherwise be eligible for sale to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must supply all Mortgage data requested by Loan Selling Advisor (in the fields
elements.label: text

elements.text: titled “Loan Fields Required for Pricing” and “Additional Loan Fields That Affect Pricing”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: so that a Transferee Servicer may provide an accurate bid for the assignment and assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Servicing Contract Rights. Failure to provide all Mortgage data requested by Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor may result in a subsequent rescission of the Transferee Servicer’s bid. In that
elements.label: text

elements.text: event, if there is no alternative Servicing bid and the Mortgage has been allocated to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mandatory Cash Contract, the Seller will be subject to a pairoff fee as provided in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Funding adjustments and net funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: Funding adjustments are comprised of the Escrow funds, interest adjustment, Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread, tax service fee, transaction fee and temporary subsidy buydown funds. The amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: of funding adjustments will be identified on the Funding Date as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller enters in Loan Selling Advisor the amounts held as Escrow funds and
elements.label: text

elements.text: temporary subsidy buydown funds for each Mortgage sold servicing-released, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Selling Advisor calculates the interest adjustment, Servicing Spread, tax service fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: and transaction fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 6302.26 for additional information regarding delivery requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold servicing-released under Mandatory Cash Contracts or Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller is responsible for the accuracy of the Escrow fund amounts and temporary subsidy
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown fund amounts that the Seller submits via Loan Selling Advisor. Freddie Mac does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not edit or validate these amounts. On the Funding Date, Freddie Mac will deduct the amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the funding adjustments from the sales proceeds paid by Freddie Mac for the purchase of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Mortgage sold servicing-released. Freddie Mac will forward the deducted amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: funding adjustments to the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller and Transferee Servicer are responsible for any reconciliation, correction or
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustment to the amount of Escrow funds or temporary subsidy buydown funds transferred
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Transferee Servicer. Freddie Mac will not adjust, calculate, refund or pay any amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to the Escrow funds or temporary subsidy buydown funds after the Funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Identification of Transferee Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller authorizes Freddie Mac to select Freddie Mac-approved Servicers as Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers to participate in Cash-Released XChange.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Excluding Transferee Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: Through Loan Selling Advisor, Freddie Mac will inform the Seller of the identity of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer(s) that may offer a price to assume the Seller’s Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Cash-Released XChange.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller has the right to exclude a Transferee Servicer from assuming the Seller’s interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Servicing Contract Rights for Mortgages sold servicing-released.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Such exclusion will not apply to Mortgages sold servicing-released for which the Seller has
elements.label: text

elements.text: already entered into a Mandatory Cash Contract or Best Efforts Contract before Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: excluded the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller re-prices a servicing-released contract, a new bid for the assumption of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s interest in the Servicing Contract Rights may be accepted. The Seller must verify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: identity of the Transferee Servicer by reviewing the Contract Confirmation that will disclose
elements.label: text

elements.text: the most current Transferee Servicer’s bid.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller has excluded a Transferee Servicer under the first paragraph of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 6101.5(f), the Seller has the right to restore that Transferee Servicer’s ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assume the Seller’s Servicing Contract Rights for Mortgages sold servicing-released.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Authorization to disclose the Seller’s identity
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller authorizes Freddie Mac to disclose and identify any Transferee Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: potential Transferee Servicer that the Seller is an eligible Seller of Mortgages and Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer for the related Transfer of Servicing Contract Rights through Cash-Released
elements.label: text

elements.text: XChange.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, Freddie Mac may, in its discretion, disclose the following information to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer that services Mortgages that the Seller has sold to Freddie Mac through
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash-Released XChange:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Seller is no longer a Freddie Mac-approved Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Seller is no longer eligible to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac through Cash￾Released XChange
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Servicer’s rights to screen out the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller acknowledges that a Transferee Servicer may terminate the Seller’s ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer the Seller’s Servicing Contract Rights to such Servicer at any time without notifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller. The Seller will have no right of action, appeal or other recourse against Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac and releases all claims it may have against Freddie Mac and/or the Servicer in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with such termination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Late fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, in its sole discretion, charge the Seller a late fee of up to $500 for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage for which the Seller:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fails to comply with the requirements in Section 4.2 of Exhibit 28A
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Incorrectly identifies all funding adjustments or fails to reconcile with the Transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer all funding adjustments as required in Section 6101.5(d) or as required in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5.1 of Exhibit 28A
elements.label: text

elements.text: These late fees are in addition to any fees that the Seller may be required to pay the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: as provided in Exhibit 28A.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages sold through Cash-Released XChange under a Mandatory Cash Contract or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Best Efforts Contract, the Seller remains solely liable to Freddie Mac for all sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations, covenants and warranties in the Purchase Documents (the “sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations and warranties”) of the Mortgages for which the related Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights are assigned to the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Transferee Servicer is liable to Freddie Mac for all Servicing duties, obligations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities, representations, covenants, warranties, agreements and related liabilities in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Documents with respect to the Mortgages related to the Concurrent Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing. See Sections 7101.3(c), (d), (e) and (f) for liabilities with respect to Subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfers of Servicing of these Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will conduct quality control activities relating to the sale representations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: warranties with the Seller. The Seller agrees that Freddie Mac may share information with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Transferee Servicer regarding the Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac servicing-released,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, quality control findings, the Seller’s response to quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: findings and defect reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Document custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers are required to deliver the Notes for Mortgages other than eMortgages that are sold
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Cash-Released XChange to a Document Custodian listed in Exhibit 43, Cash￾Released XChange® Document Custodians. See Section 2202.2(c) for the document custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Mortgages sold through Cash-Released XChange. See Chapter 1402,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 4 and the Seller’s other Purchase Documents for the document custody
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for eMortgages sold through Cash-Released XChange.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6102:ARM Cash
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 6102.1: ARM Cash Eligibility (10/01/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ARM Cash defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Sellers for ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages and purchase requirements under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) ARM Cash defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers should be familiar with the following defined terms as they relate to ARMs sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac under ARM Cash:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Attributes — For ARMs sold under an ARM Cash Contract, the Loan Attributes
elements.label: text

elements.text: are the Note Rate, Margin, Servicing Spread, Life Cap and Initial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Level Tolerance — For ARMs sold under an ARM Cash Contract, the Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tolerance is the range of values specified in the contract for the Loan Attributes of each
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM that may be allocated to the contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Sellers for ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: In order to be eligible to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac under ARM Cash, the Seller must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have entered into Purchase Contracts with Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Margin requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, at its sole discretion, require Sellers to post margin in connection with
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions under this Chapter 6102. In all events, Freddie Mac shall be the sole
elements.label: text

elements.text: calculation agent with respect to the required margin amount and whether the margin
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount is to be posted or returned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Margin deficit notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac provides notice of a margin deficit by 10:00 a.m. Eastern time on any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day, the Seller shall transfer any required margin no later than the close of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business on such Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If such notice is received after 10:00 a.m. Eastern time on any Business Day or on a day
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is not a Business Day, the Seller shall transfer such margin no later than the close of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business on the next Business Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Establishing a securities trading account
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller may establish a trading account with Freddie Mac that allows the Seller to
elements.label: text

elements.text: engage in the purchase and sale of UMBS® and Freddie Mac Mortgage Participation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: To request approval to establish such an account, Sellers may e-mail or call the Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Window at CashDesk_Traders@FreddieMac.com or (571) 382-3080.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any securities trading activities are subject to the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sellers must comply with the margining terms and credit limits set forth in Exhibit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 102, Securities Trading Customer Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will be the calculation agent for purposes of determining any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts payable pursuant to the terms of any margin provisions, and such
elements.label: text

elements.text: determinations by Freddie Mac shall be final and binding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligible Mortgages and purchase requirements under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgage variability under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under ARM Cash, the ARMs in a single contract may be delivered with Note Rates,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Margins, Life Caps, Servicing Spreads and Initial Periods that vary within a specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: range.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Accounting Net Yield (ANY) for Mortgages sold under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: An ANY will be calculated for each ARM delivered under an ARM Cash Contract and
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be based on the individual ARM’s Note Rate and Servicing Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Special eligibility requirements for Mortgages sold under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless specified otherwise in this chapter, the special eligibility requirements for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: set forth in Chapter 4401 apply to ARMs sold under ARM Cash.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Other Guide provisions related to Mortgages sold under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to the following Guide provisions for additional information related to ARMs sold
elements.label: text

elements.text: under ARM Cash:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other Guide provisions related to Mortgages sold under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide provision Guide location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Mortgage Products available
elements.label: text

elements.text: through Loan Selling Advisor®
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 17S, Available Mortgage Products
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM Note Rate change requirements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including applicable caps
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: General ARM requirements, including
elements.label: text
