elements.text: 6102.2: ARM Cash Contracts (11/05/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pricing under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Acceptance of offer under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Offer amounts and procedures for ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will endeavor to announce prior to the offer day if commitments will not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: available due to holidays or other reasons.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts under ARM Cash are mandatory. Each Business Day, a Seller may take out
elements.label: text

elements.text: contracts to sell ARMs under ARM Cash.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum amount for each ARM Cash Contract is $1,000. Freddie Mac may, at its
elements.label: text

elements.text: discretion and at any time, impose a maximum contract amount for individual ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may enter into multiple mandatory cash contracts but may not exceed $200
elements.label: text

elements.text: million in aggregate fixed-rate (see Section 6101.2(a)) and ARM commitment volume per
elements.label: text

elements.text: day. The Seller may seek an exception to this amount by contacting the Cash Desk
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Cash_Ex@FreddieMac.com or 571-382-5960).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If this limit is exceeded without prior Freddie Mac approval, Freddie Mac may pair
elements.label: text

elements.text: off the Seller’s contract(s) or may require the Seller to pair off the contract(s) at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller obligations for ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under an ARM Cash Contract, the Seller must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac that have an aggregate principal balance equal to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract amount, plus or minus the greater of $10,000 or 2.5% of the contract amount,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have completed all requirements for the sale of the Mortgages by the close of
elements.label: text

elements.text: business on the Purchase Contract Expiration Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Contract periods for ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may select a Purchase Contract period of between two and 90 days or such other
elements.label: text

elements.text: time frame as permitted by Loan Selling Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages under an ARM Cash Contract may settle one at a time; however, all
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for the sale of all Mortgages under the contract must be completed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Contract Expiration Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Pairoff process for ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event an ARM Cash Contract will not be fulfilled by delivery of Mortgages, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller may request a pairoff via Loan Selling Advisor or wait until the contract expires, at
elements.label: text

elements.text: which point Loan Selling Advisor will automatically calculate the pairoff fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The pairoff process used in Loan Selling Advisor is “two-way” and may result in either a
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee due Freddie Mac from the Seller or a fee due the Seller from Freddie Mac, depending
elements.label: text

elements.text: on market conditions. See Section 6401.1(b) for additional information regarding pairoffs
elements.label: text

elements.text: of cash Purchase Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A pairoff fee calculated and provided by Loan Selling Advisor is non-negotiable. Pairoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees will be included in the Seller’s monthly invoice and paid as set forth in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to amend the provisions of this section relating to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor pairoff process without prior notice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) ARM Cash Contract extensions
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the contract amount specified in a Mandatory ARM Cash Contract will not be fulfilled
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior to the contract expiration date, the Seller may extend, in Loan Selling Advisor, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM Cash Contract at any time prior to the contract expiration date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may extend the contract an unlimited number of times as long as the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: extension period does not exceed 30 days from the original contract expiration date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In exchange for the contract extension, the Seller will be assessed a fee to be paid in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 6303.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Pricing under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Loan Level Tolerance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs sold to Freddie Mac under an ARM Cash Contract may have Loan Attributes that
elements.label: text

elements.text: vary within a specified range. The Loan Attributes are the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Life Caps
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Margin
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Numbers of months to the next Interest Change Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: When taking out an ARM Cash Contract, the Seller must provide all of the data required
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Loan Selling Advisor. Freddie Mac will use the data provided by the Seller to
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish Loan Level Tolerances for each Loan Attribute.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Obtaining pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pricing for ARM Cash is available from Loan Selling Advisor each Business Day. Once
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Seller accepts and agrees to the contract price, the Seller may not increase the contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount. The Seller may take out another contract to sell additional ARMs under ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Certain negotiated Credit Fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, from time to time, assess certain negotiated Credit Fees. Any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: pricing will be made available to the Seller on the cash Purchase Contract loan-level fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: breakdown screen in Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the provisions of Section 1101.2(a)(ii), Freddie Mac may amend,
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplement, revise or terminate any pricing or provisions implemented pursuant to this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section in whole or in part upon written notice to the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Acceptance of offer under ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as provided in Section 1201.4(b), there is a binding commitment by Freddie Mac to
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase, and by the Seller to sell, ARMs under the contract terms (subject to conformity of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each ARM to the requirements of the Purchase Documents) once the Seller has accepted and
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreed to the contract price provided by Freddie Mac in Loan Selling Advisor. The terms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Purchase Contract are provided in Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6102.3: Purchase and settlement under ARM Cash
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funding Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Funding amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest paid to the Servicer in connection with Mortgage funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Funding Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: By the close of business on the Purchase Contract Expiration Date, the Seller must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed all the requirements for the sale of the Mortgages to Freddie Mac. Sellers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: request a specific Funding Date for each of the Mortgages sold under an ARM Cash Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: or elect to have funding occur as soon as practicable after the requirements for sale of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac have been met (“ASAP funding”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Funding amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: As each Mortgage funds, Freddie Mac will pay the Seller the amount calculated by
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiplying the price (including all adjustments to price based on the ARM Loan Attributes)
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in the cash Purchase Contract by the UPB of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Mortgage is subject to Credit Fees or credits for Credit Fees, Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Net the fee or credit amounts against the amount paid to the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reflect any subsequent change to Credit Fees and credits for Credit Fees on the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly invoice
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Interest paid to the Servicer in connection with Mortgage funding
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with the Mortgage funding, Freddie Mac will determine the amount of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: to pay the Servicer on each ARM purchased under ARM Cash by computing the interest on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the UPB of the ARM from the first day of the Funding Date month through the day before
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Funding Date using the Accounting Net Yield (ANY).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Thereafter, Freddie Mac will compensate the Servicer in an amount equal to the Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread for each ARM and authorizes the Servicer to retain the Servicing Spread out of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest payments made by the Borrower, and the Servicer will remit the ANY to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6102.4: Servicing options and remittance cycle for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: ARM Cash Contracts (10/01/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing options for ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance cycle and Servicing Spreads for ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing options for ARM Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject to approval and conditions set by Freddie Mac, Sellers may sell ARMs under ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cash using the following Servicing options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing retained: The Seller sells the Mortgage to Freddie Mac and agrees to service
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgages in accordance with the Guide and applicable Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Concurrent Transfer of Servicing: The Seller sells the Mortgage to Freddie Mac and
elements.label: text

elements.text: agrees to service the Mortgages in accordance with the Guide and applicable Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents and concurrently engages in a Transfer of Servicing initiated by a Seller to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer with the purchase of the Mortgage and the Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurring on the Settlement Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer selected by the Seller must be a Freddie Mac-approved Servicer eligible to
elements.label: text

elements.text: service the particular Mortgage, and Freddie Mac must approve the Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Chapter 7101.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 6302.51 for additional information regarding delivery requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold servicing-released under Mandatory Cash Contracts or Best Efforts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Remittance cycle and Servicing Spreads for ARM Cash Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Remittance cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Standard Remittance Cycle applies to Mortgages sold under ARM Cash Contracts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Minimum Contract Servicing Spreads
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicing Spread applicable to any individual Mortgage delivered pursuant to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific Purchase Contract may be greater than or equal to, but not less than, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Contract Servicing Spread selected by the Seller for that specific Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract. The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread is referred to in Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor® as servicing fee rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread is selected by the Seller when taking out a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Contract and is applicable to all the Mortgages delivered pursuant to that
elements.label: text

elements.text: specific Purchase Contract. The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be equal to or
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than the Minimum Servicing Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When entering into an ARM Cash Contract, the Seller must select a Minimum Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spread for the contract that is no less than 0.250% and no greater than 0.375%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will use the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread selected by the Seller to
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish the acceptable Servicing Spread for ARMs allocated to the contract (Loan Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tolerance).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 6201: Guarantor and MultiLender Swap
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6201.1: Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: eligibility (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Definitions and context for terms used in the chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provisions for selling eligible Mortgages to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Securities issued under the fixed-rate Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Securities issued under the WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sellers eligible to submit offers under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MultiLender Swap posting information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s obligation to sell UMBS®, MBS or ARM PCs in exchange for Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Definitions and context for terms used in the chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless the context requires otherwise:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The term “Mortgages” used in this chapter refers to fixed-rate Mortgages and ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The phrase “Guarantor program” refers to the fixed-rate Guarantor program and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The requirements of this chapter apply to the Guarantor program and the MultiLender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Swap program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Provisions for Selling eligible Mortgages to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: The provisions of this chapter set forth the terms for selling eligible Mortgages to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in exchange for UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PCs. Mortgages may be sold in exchange
elements.label: text

elements.text: for securities through one of the following purchase programs:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Fixed-rate Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conventional fixed-rate Mortgages may be sold to Freddie Mac in exchange for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS or MBS representing an undivided interest in the same Mortgages. FHA and
elements.label: text

elements.text: VA Mortgages may be sold to Freddie Mac in exchange for an MBS representing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: undivided interest in the same Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conventional ARMs may be sold to Freddie Mac in exchange for a WAC ARM PC
elements.label: text

elements.text: representing an undivided interest in the same Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ MultiLender Swap program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Eligible conventional fixed-rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conventional 10-, 15-, 20- and 30-year fixed-rate Mortgages may be sold to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in exchange for an MBS or UMBS representing an undivided interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Government Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate Government Mortgages (FHA/VA, Section 184 Native American, Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 502 GRH) may be sold to Freddie Mac in exchange for an MBS representing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: undivided interest in the same Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delivery under the Freddie Mac Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: mandatory, but delivery under Pricing Identifier Terms may be mandatory. See
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1501.2(b) for further information regarding mandatory delivery requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For additional information on the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs, refer to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following chapters in the Guide:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additional references for Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Topic Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate Guarantor Chapters 6202 and 6203
elements.label: text

elements.text: WAC ARM Guarantor Chapters 6202 and 6204
elements.label: text

elements.text: MultiLender Swap Chapters 6202 and 6205
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 6201.1(c) and 6201.1(d) summarize the types of securities issued under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guarantor program or MultiLender Swap program. For detailed information, see Exhibit 17S,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Available Mortgage Products.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For current Prefix information, contact Investor Inquiry at (800) 336-3672.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Securities issued under the fixed-rate Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The fixed-rate Guarantor program issues securities for pools of the following types of fixed￾rate Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage type Terms available Pooling rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: Conventional fixed-rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 10-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 15-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 20-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 30-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 10- and 15-year terms may be pooled
elements.label: text

elements.text: together in a 15-year security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 20- and 30-year terms may be pooled
elements.label: text

elements.text: together in a 30-year security
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA and VA Mortgages ■ 15-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 20-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 30-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: 20- and 30-year terms may be pooled
elements.label: text

elements.text: together in a 30-year security.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Securities issued under the WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: The WAC ARM Guarantor program issues WAC ARM PCs for eligible ARM products.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Separate WAC ARM PCs will be issued based on the characteristics of the ARM. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: following characteristics are used to form WAC ARM PCs:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Index
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Lookback Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Initial and Periodic Caps
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Length of Initial Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Length of subsequent adjustment periods, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether the ARMs are subject to prepayment penalties or not
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Chapter 4401 and Exhibit 17S for detailed information on the combination of ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: characteristics that are acceptable for WAC ARM PCs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Sellers eligible to submit offers under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Sellers are eligible to submit offers under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs, provided that the Seller first enters into Pricing Identifier Terms with Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller should contact its Freddie Mac Account Manager to discuss how to enter into such
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Multilender Swap posting information
elements.label: text

elements.text: On the first Business Day of each month, Freddie Mac will announce at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs/data/FRE_Multilender_Table.txt and
elements.label: text

elements.text: through various market information vendors the following information with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages Freddie Mac will purchase under the MultiLender Swap program:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage term and final Settlement Dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ WA Net Interest Rate (Coupon)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Permissible Note Rate range
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prefix
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security Identifier (Pool Number)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ CUSIP Number
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Freddie Mac’s obligation to sell UMBS, MBS or ARM PCs in exchange for Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The obligation of Freddie Mac to sell UMBS, MBS or ARM PCs in exchange for Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: is subject to the Mortgages’ eligibility for purchase, the Seller’s compliance with any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount established for the Seller and all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6201.2: Guarantor and MultiLender Swap Contracts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review of Mortgages for compliance with Seller’s Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller’s election to sell with or without recourse under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buyup and buydown programs under the fixed-rate Guarantor and MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Review of Mortgages for compliance with Seller’s Maximum Annual Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac has established a Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount with respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Seller, Freddie Mac will, after the commitment has been made, review the aggregate
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of Mortgages purchased from the Seller to date in the current calendar year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, in its sole and absolute discretion, may purchase Mortgages from the Seller in
elements.label: text

elements.text: an amount that exceeds the Seller’s Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount or may
elements.label: text

elements.text: rescind, in whole or in part, its acceptance of the Seller’s offer to sell any Mortgages that will
elements.label: text

elements.text: result in the Seller’s exceeding its Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount. Any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: rescission shall be made without the payment by Freddie Mac of any Seller claims.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to charge a fee on the entire amount of any Purchase Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: that causes the Seller to exceed its Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Seller’s election to sell with or without recourse under the Guarantor and MultiLender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages may be sold to Freddie Mac with or without recourse, as elected by the Seller and
elements.label: text

elements.text: as set forth in the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Mortgages with recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller who elects to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac with recourse bears all risks and
elements.label: text

elements.text: costs of a Borrower default, including the costs of foreclosure. Under a recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller must repurchase the Mortgage, plus accrued interest, upon completion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the foreclosure sale (or upon delivery of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure), whether or not
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower retains an equity of redemption
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller may, at its option, repurchase:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any Mortgage that is 90 days delinquent in accordance with the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any Mortgage for which foreclosure has been initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller elects to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac with recourse, the Seller’s financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: ability to fulfill the required recourse obligation will be reviewed. If Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that the Seller’s financial condition is not sufficient to support a recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation, the Seller may be required to meet additional requirements. There may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: instances in which additional recourse sales from the Seller will not be allowed because
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Seller’s financial condition.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if the Seller elects to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac with recourse, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will monitor the Seller’s continuing ability to fulfill its recourse obligations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require that the Seller submit specific financial information relating to the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: ability to fulfill its recourse obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require that the Seller provide Freddie Mac with additional assurances, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranties or pledges of collateral, to support recourse obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Treat the Seller’s failure to provide Freddie Mac with requested additional assurances
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a failure to fulfill an obligation under the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller who elects to sell Mortgages with recourse represents and warrants to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac that on the Date of Seller’s Offer, the Seller’s aggregate recourse obligations comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with all applicable federal and State regulations. The Seller should be aware that in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: event of a Transfer of Servicing, the Transferee Servicer must be approved by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac and may be subject to the same recourse obligations as the Seller of the related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac with recourse, the Note must bear the proper
elements.label: text

elements.text: endorsement for sales with recourse, as set forth in Section 6301.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Mortgages without recourse
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller elects to sell Mortgages without recourse, Freddie Mac assumes the risk of
elements.label: text

elements.text: loss from Borrower defaults, subject to the terms and conditions of the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Buyup and buydown programs under the fixed-rate Guarantor and MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Adjustments to Credit Fees in Yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: When taking out a Fixed-Rate Guarantor Contract or MultiLender Swap Contract, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller may request an adjustment to the Credit Fee in Yield by selecting one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following buyup and buydown programs:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note-level, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan-level (if permitted under the Seller’s Pricing Identifier Terms under which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller is taking out the Purchase Contract)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The adjustment will be based on the Note Rate, the Coupon, and the Minimum Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spread:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For the note-level program, the Credit Fee in Yield will be adjusted in the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner for all Mortgages with that Note Rate and remaining maturity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For the loan-level program, the Seller may select the specific Mortgages for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fee in Yield is to be adjusted
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this section, Credit Fee in Yield means the total Credit Fee in Yield,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which consists of the Credit Fee in Yield stated on the Seller’s Rate Sheet plus all
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Credit Fee in Yield Add-Ons and negotiated Credit Fee in Yield adjustments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Selection of applicable option
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the note-level program, at the time the Seller takes out a Purchase Contract, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must select one of the following options:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buyup and buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buydown only
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buyup only, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Neither buyup nor buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the loan-level program, the Seller will select the applicable option for each Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time the Seller delivers the Mortgage. See Section 6302.35 for delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for such Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller fails to deliver the required loan-level buyup and buydown ULDD Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Points for a Mortgage, the Mortgage will not be allocated to the Seller’s Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For both the note-level and loan-level programs, the Seller may update the specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: buyup or buydown amount, as applicable, until the Purchase Contract for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buyup or buydown amount applies is in settlement locked status.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Buyup and buydown fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: In exchange for an increase to the Credit Fee in Yield (buyup) for certain Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will make a cash payment to the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In exchange for a decrease in the Credit Fee in Yield (buydown) for certain Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller will make a cash payment to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Buyup payments to be made to the Seller will be netted against buydown fees to be paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Seller and the result will be paid in accordance with the provisions relating to fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 6303.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Calculation of buyup proceeds and buydown fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will determine the amount of the increase or decrease in the Credit Fee in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield for each Mortgage delivered based on the following information specified when
elements.label: text

elements.text: taking out a Purchase Contract:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coupon for which the Mortgage is delivered
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum Contract Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Credit Fee in Yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buyup maximum amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: In determining the amount of any increase or decrease in the Credit Fee in Yield, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will use the following formula:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate -
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: Total
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fee in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: - Coupon =
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amount of
elements.label: text

elements.text: increase or
elements.label: text

elements.text: decrease in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fee in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield *
elements.label: text

elements.text: * Expressed in basis points
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the result is a positive number: The Credit Fee in Yield will be increased by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of that positive number, subject to the maximum buyup limit prevailing at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the Purchase Contract is taken out. Any Mortgage with a positive number greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the maximum buyup limit will retain a Servicing Spread greater than the Minimum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Servicing Spread specified at the time the Purchase Contract is taken out.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the result is a negative number: The Credit Fee in Yield will be decreased by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of that negative number, subject to the maximum buydown limit prevailing at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time the Purchase Contract is taken out but will not be decreased to less than zero. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Rate must equal or exceed the Coupon plus the Credit Fee in Yield (as adjusted for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amount of the buyup or buydown) plus the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: accepted for purchase for the Coupon specified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Calculation of buyup and buydown fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Buyup and buydown fees will be calculated in accordance with the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each basis point (0.010%) by which the Credit Fee in Yield for a Mortgage must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: increased or decreased as determined by the preceding formula, a payment will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in an amount equal to a fixed percent (expressed in basis points) of the UPB of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The number of basis points determined by Freddie Mac will depend on:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Note Rate,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage product, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Whether a buyup or buydown is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Application of buyup maximum amount for Note Rate program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages are subject to the buyup maximum amount applicable for Mortgage products
elements.label: text

elements.text: as stated in the Seller’s Purchase Documents. The Seller may enter a lower buyup
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum amount to be applied to the Fixed-Rate Guarantor Contract or MultiLender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Swap Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller does not enter a value into the buyup maximum field for a Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract, Loan Selling Advisor® will apply the applicable buyup maximum amount as
elements.label: text

elements.text: stated in the Seller’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Buyup and buydown options under WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: The buyup and buydown options are not permitted under WAC ARM Guarantor
elements.label: text

elements.text: program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Payment of buyup proceeds and buydown fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: All buyup proceeds and buydown fees will be paid in accordance with Section 6303.2(a).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For general billing information, call Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6201.3: Guarantor and MultiLender Swap pricing,
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: purchase and settlement (12/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Guarantor Pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security wire instructions for the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Settlement under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Postsettlement adjustments for Mortgages purchased under the Guarantor and MultiLender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Guarantor Pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section applies when a Seller’s Rate Sheet (as defined below) issued through Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor® states that the Seller is eligible to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac through
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guarantor program or MultiLender Swap program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of this section, the following terms have the meanings ascribed to them
elements.label: text

elements.text: below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to guarantor pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: G
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guarantor Pricing Credit Fees in Yield, Credit Fee in Yield Add-Ons, negotiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fee in Yield adjustments, buyup and buydown ratios,
elements.label: text

elements.text: maximum buyup and maximum buydown, and/or certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: negotiated Credit Fees, credits for Credit Fees and/or such other
elements.label: text

elements.text: components as Freddie Mac may include from time to time
elements.label: text

elements.text: P
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pricing Day The day or days of the month on which Guarantor Pricing is
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to the Seller in Loan Selling Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: R
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate Sheet The Rate Sheet Results screen in Loan Selling Advisor that
elements.label: text

elements.text: contains the information described in Section 6201.3(a)(ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Pricing Day and pricing notification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Pricing Day will be stated on the Seller’s Rate Sheet. If the Pricing Day is not a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day, Freddie Mac will make the Guarantor Pricing available to the Seller on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first Business Day after such Pricing Day.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Rate Sheet will include all applicable components of Guarantor Pricing, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage products eligible under the Rate Sheet and the applicable effective dates and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlements Dates covered by the Rate Sheet and any applicable additional terms stated in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Business Arrangement Stipulations section of the Rate Sheet.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Taking out Purchase Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may take out Purchase Contracts for settlement in a particular month only after
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has entered Guarantor Pricing on the Rate Sheet for the Purchase Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Settlement in such particular month.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Acceptance of Guarantor Pricing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s delivery of Mortgages eligible under a Rate Sheet will be deemed to be the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s acceptance of the Guarantor Pricing stated in the Rate Sheet.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Amendment and termination of Guarantor Pricing provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the provisions of Section 1101.2(a)(ii), Freddie Mac may amend,
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplement, revise or terminate any of the provisions of this section, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, establishing Guarantor Pricing for a Seller more than one time in a calendar
elements.label: text

elements.text: month and changing the Guarantor Pricing provided in accordance with the above
elements.label: text

elements.text: provision, in whole or in part, upon written notice to the Seller. The written notice may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be in the form of new Guarantor Pricing being made available to the Seller on the Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sheet in Loan Selling Advisor. The effective date of changes will be stated on the revised
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate Sheet. The changes will not apply to contracts the Seller has taken out prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of the changes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Security wire instructions for the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Acknowledgment and consent to electronic transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with Chapter 1401, the Seller is bound by the security wire instructions it
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Security wire verification
elements.label: text

elements.text: For information about wire transfer instructions and delivery of UMBS®, MBS or ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: PCs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security wire verification instructions and delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: Type of program Guide section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fixed-rate Guarantor program Section 6203.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: WAC ARM Guarantor program Section 6204.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: MultiLender Swap program Section 6205.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac is not liable for errors in the delivery of UMBS, MBS or ARM PCs provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: that Freddie Mac delivered the securities pursuant to security wire instructions that were
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided by the Seller in accordance with the requirements of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Settlement under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Settlement Date, ownership of, and (subject to the terms of any recourse obligation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: risk of loss with respect to, the Mortgages being sold on the Settlement Date will pass from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac will, in accordance with Section 6201.6(c), deliver to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller in Book-Entry Form the UMBS, MBS or ARM PCs representing interests therein
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the amount determined by Freddie Mac and verified by the Seller in the Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor “View Wire Instructions” screen.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Postsettlement credits and calculations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will credit or remit to the Seller on a postsettlement basis the sum of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An amount of interest equal to one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text:  With respect to Pools without the Note-level buyup and buydown feature, interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the Credit Fee in Yield on the Mortgages for the period from the Issue Date to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the day before the Settlement Date, inclusive
elements.label: text

elements.text:  With respect to Pools in which the Note-level buyup and buydown feature has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been used, the weighted average Credit Fee in Yield of the Mortgages in the Pool
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the period from the Issue Date to the day before the Settlement Date, inclusive
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The excess, if any, of the aggregate UPB as of the Settlement Date of the Mortgages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: over the aggregate original principal balance of the related UMBS, MBS or ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: PCs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The sum will be credited against any fees payable by the Seller to Freddie Mac for
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly invoiced fees, including, but not limited to, buydown fees, Gold Rush® fees and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Fees. If this sum exceeds postsettlement fees, a net amount is payable to the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment of the net amount due to the Seller will be made in accordance with the Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fee and funding fee provisions stated in Chapter 6303.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Failure of transfer or purchase obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller fails for any reason to transfer all or any portion of the Mortgages accepted
elements.label: text

elements.text: for purchase by Freddie Mac, Freddie Mac will have no obligation to sell all or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: portion of the UMBS, MBS or ARM PCs to the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller fails for any reason to purchase all or any portion of the UMBS, MBS or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM PCs from Freddie Mac, Freddie Mac will have no obligation to purchase all or any
elements.label: text

elements.text: portion of the Mortgages from the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Postsettlement adjustments for Mortgages purchased under the Guarantor and
elements.label: text

elements.text: MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Transfer of ownership for unfunded Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, after any Settlement Date, Freddie Mac determines that the aggregate UPB, as of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Settlement Date, of the Mortgages purchased on such date (the “settlement balance”) was
elements.label: text

elements.text: greater than the aggregate UPB verified for purchase (the “verified balance”) and greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: than the original principal balance of the related UMBS, MBS or ARM PC, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will transfer back to the Seller ownership of the smallest number of Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“unfunded Mortgages”) having an aggregate UPB as of the Settlement Date at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: equal to the excess of the settlement balance over the verified balance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting and remitting of excess amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the aggregate UPB of the unfunded Mortgages transferred back to the Seller exceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: the difference between the settlement balance and the verified balance, the excess must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reported and remitted promptly by the Seller to Freddie Mac in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8302.3(e) and 8303.2(b) as if such difference were a principal prepayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Mortgage paid in full) on the related Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Correction of verified balance discrepancies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, the Seller may not remedy an incorrect
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified balance by offsetting any difference between the settlement balance and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: verified balance against principal collections received on the Mortgages by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Settlement Date. The amount of principal reduction reported by the Seller via
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Loan-Level Transaction (or any other report developed for reporting accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: information) must, for each accounting reporting period, accurately reflect all principal
elements.label: text

elements.text: collections actually received by the Seller on the verified balance plus any prepayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: resulting from the transfer of unfunded Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Options for differences in settlement and verified balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, after the Settlement Date, Freddie Mac determines that the settlement balance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages purchased on such date was less than the verified balance and less than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original principal balance of the related UMBS, MBS or ARM PC, the Seller must, at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s option and within the time period and in the manner specified by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remit the amount of any such difference to Freddie Mac as a principal payment with
elements.label: text

elements.text: respect to such Mortgages, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deliver additional Mortgages that would have been eligible for inclusion in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: related Pool and that have an aggregate UPB at least equal to the difference between
elements.label: text

elements.text: the verified balance and the settlement balance

elements.text: 6201.4: Guarantor and MultiLender Swap program
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicing options and remittance (12/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remittance cycles under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase requirements for Mortgages sold under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Remittance cycles under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Standard Remittance Cycle applies to Mortgages sold under the Guarantor and
elements.label: text

elements.text: MultiLender Swap programs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Purchase requirements for Mortgages sold under the Guarantor and MultiLender
elements.label: text

elements.text: Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac under the Guarantor program and the MultiLender Swap
elements.label: text

elements.text: program must meet the requirements set forth in the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Fixed-rate Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under the fixed-rate Guarantor program, Freddie Mac will purchase fixed-rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that have a Note Rate at least equal to the requested Coupon plus the Credit Fee in Yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: (as adjusted for buyup or buydown) plus the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For fixed-rate Guarantor:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be at least 0.250%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages sold under fixed-rate Guarantor the maximum Servicing Spread, may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceed 0.500%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be equal to or greater than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Mortgages with lender-paid mortgage insurance that have annual or monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: renewal premiums, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be at least 0.250%
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Include the amount necessary to pay the mortgage insurance premium when due,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Not exceed 0.500%
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) MultiLender Swap program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under the MultiLender Swap program, Freddie Mac will purchase fixed-rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: that have a Note Rate (as adjusted for buyup or buydown, and inclusive of any lender￾paid mortgage insurance premium) within the specified posted Note Rate range for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For MultiLender Swap:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be at least 0.250%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum Servicing Spread may not exceed 0.500%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be equal to or greater than the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Minimum Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 4401.1(b) for Margin requirements for SOFR-indexed ARMs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For WAC ARM Guarantor, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must be at least
elements.label: text

elements.text: 0.250% and may not exceed 2.00%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For ARMs with lender-paid mortgage insurance that have annual or monthly renewal
elements.label: text

elements.text: premiums, the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread must include the amount necessary to
elements.label: text

elements.text: pay the mortgage insurance premium when due.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Discrete Minimum Contract Servicing Spread types
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, under the WAC ARM Guarantor program, the Seller selects a value for
elements.label: text

elements.text: three discrete Minimum Contract Servicing Spreads:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum Coupon Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum Margin Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum Lifetime Ceiling Servicing Spread
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject to the requirements below, the Seller may select the same value or a different
elements.label: text

elements.text: value for any of the three discrete Minimum Contract Servicing Spreads. When the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller selects a different value for any of the three discrete Minimum Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Spreads, the affected ARMs are referred to as having variable servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: spreads.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) Requirements for each spread type
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements for each spread type are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum coupon servicing spread: The minimum coupon servicing spread is
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread applicable to an ARM from the date that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchases the ARM and during the period of time that the adjusted
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note Rate is limited by the Initial Cap or Periodic Cap, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum coupon servicing spread may not vary by more than 0.250% from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the minimum margin servicing spread and the minimum lifetime ceiling servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum margin servicing spread: The minimum margin servicing spread is
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread applicable to an ARM from the first time
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the adjusted Note Rate is equal to the sum of the Index value plus the Margin.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum margin servicing spread may not vary by more than 0.250% from
elements.label: text

elements.text: the minimum coupon servicing spread and the minimum lifetime ceiling servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: spread.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimum lifetime ceiling servicing spread: The minimum lifetime ceiling
elements.label: text

elements.text: servicing spread is the Minimum Contract Servicing Spread applicable to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARM, from the first time that the adjusted Note Rate is limited by the Lifetime
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ceiling through the remaining term of the ARM.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The minimum lifetime ceiling servicing spread may not vary by more than
elements.label: text

elements.text: 0.250% from the minimum coupon servicing spread and the minimum margin
elements.label: text

elements.text: servicing spread.

elements.text: This chapter sets forth the pooling and disclosure requirements to ensure that eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivered to Freddie Mac under the Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs are pooled in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner that promotes homogeneity and disclosure transparency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Fixed-rate Guarantor program (UMBS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each UMBS or MBS Pool issued under the fixed-rate Guarantor program must consist of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages acquired from a single Seller in exchange for a UMBS or MBS representing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: undivided interest in the same Mortgages. UMBS or MBS bear interest at the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Coupon and have an original principal balance rounded down to the nearest dollar.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to selling Mortgages in exchange for UMBS or MBS with Coupons that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: evenly divisible by 0.5%, a Seller may sell 15- and 30-year fixed-rate Mortgages in exchange
elements.label: text

elements.text: for MBS with Coupons not divisible by 0.5%, provided that before selling such Mortgages to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, the Seller must obtain Freddie Mac’s written approval by contacting its Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac representative or Customer Service at 800-FREDDIE.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) MultiLender Swap program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each UMBS or MBS Pool issued under the MultiLender Swap program must consist of
elements.label: text

elements.text: fixed-rate Mortgages that Freddie Mac has acquired from one or more Sellers in exchange for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a UMBS or MBS representing an undivided interest in the same Mortgages. UMBS or MBS
elements.label: text

elements.text: bear interest at the applicable Coupon and have an original principal balance rounded down
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the nearest dollar.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) WAC ARM Guarantor program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each WAC ARM PC Pool issued under the WAC ARM Guarantor program consists of
elements.label: text

elements.text: ARMs acquired from a single Seller in exchange for a WAC ARM PC that represents an
elements.label: text

elements.text: undivided interest in the same Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Other provisions related to Guarantor and MultiLender Swap programs
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: and MultiLender Swap programs:

elements.text: 6202.2: Freddie Mac UMBS®
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: , MBS and WAC ARM PC
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosure (09/03/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ UMBS® and MBS disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ WAC ARM PC disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) UMBS and MBS disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s UMBS and MBS disclosure includes, in addition to the UMBS/MBS Offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: Circular, original Pool level information, which is current information as of the Issue Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS or MBS Pool level information is updated monthly to reflect the Mortgages in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS or MBS Pool paying off. The monthly updates for the UMBS or MBS Pool level
elements.label: text

elements.text: information may be accessed at http://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For pools issued in December 2005 and later, Freddie Mac provides loan level information
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each of the Mortgages backing Freddie Mac UMBS or MBS based on information
elements.label: text

elements.text: furnished by the Sellers; the loan level information may be accessed at
elements.label: text

elements.text: http://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For pools requiring Additional Supplements, Freddie Mac will post Additional Supplements
elements.label: text

elements.text: on its website and can furnish them upon request for the convenience of investors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a complete listing and description of the disclosure data for UMBS or MBS that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac provides for eligible fixed-rate Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac under the Guarantor
elements.label: text

elements.text: or, in certain cases, MultiLender Swap program, refer to the Disclosure Guide at
elements.label: text

elements.text: http://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs/docs/disclosure_guide.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) WAC ARM PC disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s WAC ARM PC disclosure includes, in addition to the ARM PC Offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: Circular, original WAC ARM PC Pool level information, which is current information as of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Issue Date. This information is updated monthly to reflect the Mortgages in a WAC ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: PC Pool paying off. The monthly updates for the WAC ARM PC Pool level information may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be accessed at http://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For pools issued in December 2005 and later, Freddie Mac provides loan level information
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each of the Mortgages backing Freddie Mac WAC ARM PCs based on information
elements.label: text

elements.text: furnished by the Sellers; the loan level information may be accessed at
elements.label: text

elements.text: http://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For pools requiring Additional Supplements, Freddie Mac will post Additional Supplements
elements.label: text

elements.text: on its website and can furnish them upon request for the convenience of investors.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a complete listing and description of the disclosure data for WAC ARM PCs that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac provides for eligible adjustable-rate Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: WAC ARM Guarantor program, refer to the Disclosure Guide at
elements.label: text

elements.text: http://capitalmarkets.freddiemac.com/mbs/docs/disclosure_guide.pdf.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section outlines the pooling and disclosure requirements related to issuing UMBS and MBS
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the fixed-rate Guarantor program and the MultiLender Swap program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Term requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage term requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage type Term requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 30-year UMBS or MBS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pool
elements.label: text

elements.text: The underlying fixed-rate Mortgages in each 30-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS and MBS Pool must have original terms greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 15 years (180 months) but not exceeding 30 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: (360 months).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: 20-year (240-month) fixed-rate Mortgages may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be pooled with 30-year (360-month) fixed-rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: without limit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 20-year UMBS or MBS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pool
elements.label: text

elements.text: The underlying fixed-rate Mortgages in each 20-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS and MBS Pool must have an original term greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 15 years (180 months) but not exceeding 20 years
elements.label: text

elements.text: (240 months).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 15-year UMBS or MBS
elements.label: text

elements.text: Pool
elements.label: text

elements.text: The underlying fixed-rate Mortgages in each 15-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS and MBS Pool must have original terms greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: than 84 months but not exceeding 15 years (180 months).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: 10-year (120-month) fixed-rate Mortgages may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be pooled with 15-year (180-month) fixed-rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: without limit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 10-year UMBS Pool The underlying fixed-rate Mortgages in each 10-year
elements.label: text

elements.text: UMBS Pool must have an original term greater than 84
elements.label: text

elements.text: months but not exceeding 10 years (120 months).
